Central America & Caribbean.name: Amber Cove
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Discovering new sights is one of the beauties of travel, and that couldn't be more true when it comes to Amber Cove®, Dominican Republic. In this world full of information on just about everything, Amber Cove is a throwback — it’s the kind of quiet little spot you won’t find on most maps. But you will find it — brimming with bars and pools, plus opportunities for lounging and shopping — to be the gateway to island paradise, Dominican-style. Beyond Amber Cove is Puerto Plata, a town that began life as the first European settlement in the Americas. Its founder? Oh, just a guy named Christopher Columbus… so you know that arriving by sea is the ultimate way to get here. Whether you're looking for a day at the beach, some Dominican Republic adventure or just a little poolside me-time, Amber Cove is just the ticket.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Antigua
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Discover your own slice of paradise — the white-sand kind — when you sail on cruises to Antigua. Here, legend has it, there’s actually a beach for every day of the year. (Leap years will have to share a little.) When you cruise to Antigua, located right in the heart of the Caribbean, you can’t help but notice relics of the island’s British colonial past — high tea is an afternoon ritual and cricket is the local passion, but this independent nation’s sunny island vibe reigns supreme. Cruises to Antigua are a great way to immerse yourself in all of it — the charming English culture and the irresistible Caribbean sunshine. Best of all? A Carnival cruise to Antigua makes it all a breeze. Got all that, chap?

Central America & Caribbean.name: Aruba
Central America & Caribbean.overview: It’s hard to find a more beautiful vacation destination than the Southern Caribbean, and on our cruises to Aruba, you’re the star of that perfect snapshot. Splash and play your way across this sparkling corner of the Caribbean, or simply relax on a sun-baked beach on one of Carnival’s cruises to Aruba. The locals call it the “happy island,” and they prove it — Aruba cheerily welcomes visitors to picture-perfect strips of beach, swaths of exotic blooms, and some of the Caribbean’s top shopping and nightlife locales. Celebrate the year-round Caribbean spirit, or simply unwind to the island’s bewitching and relaxing tempo. It’s easy to find your own tropical beat when you cruise Aruba-style.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Barbados
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Barbados cruises give you the chance to experience tropical life with a refined twist. With pearly pink sand and a jaunty British lilt, Barbados is one of the Caribbean’s most captivating islands. Whether you’re cruising to or from Barbados, discover a hothouse landscape where old sugar plantations and rum distilleries dot the verdant sun-dappled hills. Or, start your travels here and give yourself more time to get immersed in the local culture while you vacation in Barbados. Spy a green monkey in a rainforest ravine, sample savory local rum, or simply follow the trail of bougainvillea blossoms to a glorious beach with Carnival cruises to Barbados.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Basseterre
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Basseterre, the capital of St. Kitts and Nevis, is a bustling port city known for its vibrant mix of history, culture, and natural beauty. Situated along the island’s southeastern coast, Basseterre welcomes visitors with colorful colonial buildings, lively markets, and picturesque waterfront views. 

 As the commercial and cultural hub of St. Kitts, the city offers a unique blend of Caribbean charm and colonial architecture. Basseterre’s central location makes it a perfect base for exploring the island’s beaches, historical sites, and lush landscapes, all within easy reach.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Belize City
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Belize City, the largest city in Belize, is the country's primary port and economic hub. This vibrant city is located on the Caribbean Coast and combines urban energy with tropical charm, attracting visitors with its colorful streets, lively markets, and diverse dining scene.
Belize City is the gateway to many of the country's top attractions, including the Belize Barrier Reef, Mayan ruins, and tropical rainforests. Despite its bustling pace, the city retains a relaxed atmosphere, uniquely blending modern Belizean culture and natural beauty.

There are plenty of reasons why a cruise to Belize should top your travel bucket list. A tropical paradise that promises adventure, both onshore and off? You better Belize it! Crystalline rivers flow through impressive lowland jungles, home to wildlife of various stripes. (And various spots.) Silky-sand beaches, waving palms and colorful coral gardens are just part of Belize’s natural wonders. And they’re all yours to explore when you take a cruise to Belize, where surprises await around every corner, including underwater. Whether mountain biking through a lush rainforest or snorkeling with the marine life, you’ll experience things you can only imagine… that is, if you haven’t tried a Belize vacation yet.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Bermuda
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Kick back amidst the sun-kissed pleasures of a pink-sand paradise on Carnival cruises to King’s Wharf. This British overseas territory of Bermuda has a rich naval heritage… plus powdery beaches fringed in coconut palms. Located at the very tip of Bermuda’s glittering archipelago of over 100 islets and cays, King’s Wharf was made for lounging in the Gulf Stream’s balmy subtropical clime, which meanders between Bermuda and mainland North America.

An enchanting land of pink sand and turquoise waters — discover that paradise is in fact a reality. Bermuda may be so British that the police are constables and the countries share a royal family, but the traditional Bermudan breakfast is codfish, potato and banana — yep, this island puts its own spin on tradition. Sail on Carnival's Bermuda cruises and you'll experience this unique cultural cocktail, along with a serious helping of natural beauty. This palette of an island, combining green hills, pastel-colored homes, and pink-sand beaches makes the perfect backdrop for world-class golf, glass-bottom boats, swimming with dolphins, mountain biking and shopping. And thanks to the mild, comfortable climate, tea time's an outdoor event, year-round. Treat yourself to a getaway that's both proper and perfectly relaxed when you choose one of Carnival's cruises to Bermuda.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Bimini
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Are there words more fun to say than “I’m cruising to Bimini!”? The name rolls off the tongue delightfully, for sure… but the whole phrase means you’re in for a beautiful, uniquely chill time in this island chain. The center of the action is Alice Town, though “action” is relative when it comes to these westernmost islands of The Bahamas. The local aesthetics, however, will take your breath away — and fishing, diving and relaxing on a beach are a few of the choice activities for experiencing the naturally azure waters surrounding Bimini, which are great enjoyed from a distance or up-close!

Central America & Caribbean.name: Blowing Point Harbour
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Blowing Point Harbour is Anguilla’s main port and a popular gateway connecting the island with neighboring St. Martin. Known for its vibrant coastal beauty, this harbor is lined with white sandy beaches meeting the crystal-clear Caribbean Sea, offering an inviting tropical ambiance. 

 Visitors can enjoy panoramic views of the turquoise waters while exploring beachside eateries, local shops, and nearby resorts. The area serves as both a transportation hub for ferries and charters and a serene spot for travelers seeking relaxation in an authentic Caribbean setting.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Bonaire
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Bonaire is an island nation in the Southern Caribbean that was twice “discovered” — once by the Spanish and once by the Dutch — and was until recently part of the Netherlands Antilles. Its far-flung history may be all over the map… but you’ll find the island itself located just right. Also just right is the climate — the annual average is 81.5° F — so you’ll have plenty of reasons to spend time outdoors. When in the water, diving and snorkeling happen to be some of the best ways to see the reef surrounding the island… but they’re also great for cooling off.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Bridgetown
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Bridgetown, the vibrant capital of Barbados, offers an enticing blend of tropical beauty and historical charm. Known for its lively atmosphere, this coastal city combines Caribbean culture with remnants of British colonial architecture. Key areas like Broad Street and Swan Street are bustling with shops, local vendors, and duty-free outlets, ideal for both shopping and experiencing the city’s authentic vibe. 

 Nearby, the picturesque Careenage Marina invites leisurely strolls along the waterfront. As a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Bridgetown’s heritage sites, vibrant streets, and close proximity to beautiful beaches make it a compelling destination for any visitor.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Cancún
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Cancún, located on Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula, is a world-renowned destination known for its pristine white-sand beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and vibrant nightlife. 

 As the gateway to the Caribbean, Cancún attracts millions of visitors each year with its luxury resorts, shopping, and access to nearby Mayan ruins like Tulum and Chichén Itzá. The city’s Hotel Zone offers a perfect blend of relaxation and excitement, making it ideal for both adventure seekers and those looking to unwind.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Caribbean
Central America & Caribbean.overview: We don’t have proof, but evidence suggests that the Caribbean was made for cruising. This evidence is all around you — you’ll find in the Caribbean air, the sand and the water. And with more than 5,000 islands and cays spread across this amazing region, there’s a lot of paradise to see. So how do you choose where to visit on a Caribbean cruise? We recommend you just go and see for yourself! Best of all, the mild climate means it doesn’t even matter what time of year you go. A Carnival Caribbean cruise takes you to some of the coolest little hotspots… stretching across the world’s designated hotspot.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Cartagena
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Cartagena, officially known as Cartagena de Indias, is a vibrant port city on Colombia's northern Caribbean coast. Renowned for its well-preserved colonial architecture, the city's historic walled center and expansive fortresses have earned it UNESCO World Heritage status. 

 Beyond its historical allure, Cartagena boasts modern districts like Bocagrande, featuring upscale hotels, restaurants, and shopping centers, all set against picturesque beaches. This blend of old-world charm and contemporary amenities makes Cartagena a captivating destination for travelers seeking both cultural enrichment and seaside relaxation.

If you guessed that Cartagena’s name comes from the Tunisian Carthage, give yourself one history point. This “New Carthage” was founded in the third century B.C., and has continuously been a major naval port ever since. The city’s an enduring symbol of Roman dominance, with Punic walls and an amphitheater that you can still visit today. Don’t miss the Old City neighborhood near the port, but make sure to venture further to get the full experience — regional delights like Murcia’s 17th century Sanctuary of Fuensanta, the Moorish-built gardens of Huerto del Cura, and Lorca Castle’s Fortress of the Sun, all reward curious explorers of the Costa Calida area.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Castries
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Castries, the lively capital of Saint Lucia, is a blend of bustling markets, vibrant neighborhoods, and picturesque harbors. Located on the northwest coast, the city serves as Saint Lucia's main port, welcoming travelers with its beautiful deep-water harbor. 

 Known for attractions like the Castries Central Market and Derek Walcott Square, Castries also offers nearby sandy beaches and scenic hills. This compact city is filled with Caribbean charm, allowing visitors to enjoy everything from shopping and dining to cultural landmarks within a few days.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Celebration Key™
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Set sail for Celebration Key – your key to paradise! Unwind with island-style fun, relaxation, and adventure. Book your Carnival Cruise today!

Central America & Caribbean.name: Cockburn Town
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Grand Turk, the capital of the Turks and Caicos Islands, is a blend of rich history and natural beauty. Founded by Bermudian settlers in 1681, it became a key salt trade hub, reflected in the charming colonial architecture of Cockburn Town. 

 Known as Christopher Columbus's likely first landing point in the New World, the island also marked a significant moment in the Space Race when astronaut John Glenn first touched land after orbiting Earth in 1962. Today, Grand Turk entices visitors with its stunning beaches, vibrant culture, and exceptional diving opportunities, including some of the Caribbean's best coral reefs.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Colón
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Colón, located on the Caribbean coast of Panama, is a vibrant city that serves as the gateway to the Panama Canal’s Atlantic side. Known for its bustling port, the city is a commercial hub with a rich cultural heritage. Visitors can explore its lively streets, colorful markets, and diverse cuisine, reflecting influences from Afro-Caribbean and Panamanian cultures. 

 Despite its modern-day activity, Colón retains an old-world charm in its architecture and local traditions. Its proximity to pristine beaches and lush rainforests makes it a fantastic destination for travelers.

Some see Colón as the gateway city to the Panama Canal, the engineering wonder linking the Atlantic and Pacific oceans. Others know it as the second-largest duty-free port in all of the Caribbean, and a great base for exploring the canal and Panama’s nearby ecological treasures. On your Carnival cruise to Colón, you’ll see it from all sides — enjoy a side of shop-‘till-you-drop as you spy the brilliant plumage of a toucan in rainforest greenery.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Cristóbal
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Cristóbal, located within the vibrant district of Colón, Panama, is a historic port town known for its strategic position at the Atlantic entrance of the Panama Canal. With its rich blend of Caribbean charm and colonial influences, Cristóbal is a gateway to exploring Panama's cultural diversity. 

 The town boasts colorful architecture, bustling markets, and scenic coastal views, making it a lively destination for visitors. As a hub for maritime trade, Cristóbal offers a mix of historic landmarks, modern amenities, and easy access to nearby natural attractions.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Curaçao
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises to Curacao take you into the exciting pulse of the Southern Caribbean. Curaçao is where Europe meets the West Indies, and it collides here beautifully in a smashing blend of bold colors and cosmopolitan culture. The largest of what was once the Netherlands Antilles, this Caribbean island has it all. Cruise to Curaçao for the beautiful beaches and historic architecture, but don’t miss everything from the splashy sights of marine parks to the distinctive local liqueur — it all awaits on cruises to Curaçao. Above all, what awaits you is the warm, welcoming embrace you’ll start to feel the minute you set sail to Curaçao.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Dominica
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises to Dominica are full of nature’s best, and they feature some of the Caribbean’s most vivid waterfalls, hot springs, mountains and streams... all bubbling with life. Thrill to the sweeping grandeur of this verdant island, where wispy clouds swirl around velvety mountains and the air is alive with birdcall. On your Carnival cruise to Dominica, go wild in the lush terrain on a rainforest hike or a go native in a waterfall pool. You’ll spot flora and fauna of every brilliant rainbow color on cruises to Dominica, from the iridescent peacocks in Papillote Gardens to giant ferns along jungle trails.

Central America & Caribbean.name: El Guamache
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Margarita Island, located in the Caribbean Sea, is part of Venezuela's Nueva Esparta state. Renowned for its pristine beaches, tropical climate, and vibrant culture, it is a favorite destination for travelers seeking sun-soaked relaxation and adventure. The island features a mix of bustling towns, serene fishing villages, and modern tourist hubs. 

 Visitors can enjoy water sports, explore diverse dining options, and immerse themselves in a lively nightlife scene. Margarita's duty-free status also makes it a shopping haven, offering excellent value for souvenirs and luxury goods.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Ensenada
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Thanks to its stunning coastline, Ensenada will give you plenty of postcard-worthy moments…, and it couldn’t be easier to get there. Escape to Mexico’s original seaside getaway with Carnival cruises to Ensenada. Once a favorite hideaway for high-rolling Hollywood stars, Ensenada is now one of Mexico’s most popular ports of call. You can sightsee, shop or kayak at Punta Banda… but the true pleasures of this festive city are culinary. That’s why a cruise to Ensenada, Mexico should top every foodie’s wish list. Have your fill of fresh oysters and sip a rich Baja merlot. Soak up the richness of the local culture as you enjoy the journey.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Falmouth
Central America & Caribbean.overview: For a cruise to Jamaica with a leisurely pace and plenty of natural beauty, make sure cruises to Falmouth top your list. This island nation is famously laid-back and one of the easiest ways to enjoy that relaxed rhythm would definitely be a Falmouth, Jamaica cruise! The plantation days of Falmouth may be gone, but what remains is the largest collection of intact Colonial Georgian architecture in the Caribbean, set on sand-dusted streets and a wind-swept harbor. On Carnival cruises to Falmouth you can explore the great houses of old sugar plantations and kick back on a crescent of white-sand beach, all in one day. Get into the groove and find out more about Carnival cruises to Jamaica.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Fort-de-France
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Fort-de-France, the capital of Martinique, is a vibrant city located on the island’s west coast along Fort-de-France Bay. As the island’s largest city and cultural hub, Fort-de-France combines French elegance with Caribbean charm. Visitors can enjoy colorful markets, beautiful architecture, and scenic views of the bay. 

 Key attractions include La Savane Park, a large green space with gardens, and the historic Fort Saint Louis. Known for its lively ambiance, Fort-de-France also offers diverse dining, shopping, and nightlife, making it an ideal destination for cultural immersion and relaxation.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Frederiksted
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Frederiksted, located on the tranquil western coast of St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands, is a charming Caribbean port town known for its stunning sunsets and historical architecture. Often referred to as "Freedom City," it offers visitors a relaxed atmosphere, with pastel-colored buildings, colonial-style architecture, and pristine beaches. 

 Its laid-back vibe attracts those looking to explore local culture and unwind. The town is popular for its cruise ship dock, making it a vibrant stop for travelers. Rich in both history and natural beauty, Frederiksted is a must-visit for those seeking an authentic island experience.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Freeport
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruise to Freeport, Bahamas for the ultimate white-sand beach, crystalline-water daydream come to life. The action is sizzling in Freeport, the resort center of Grand Bahama Island. You’ll find plenty of razzle-dazzle entertainment, from a casino to music clubs to barefoot beach bars. If that’s the Bahamas you’ve come for, great! But if you’re looking for the Bahamas with the seductive, palm-fringed beaches, gentle turquoise seas, and nature at its most vivid… Freeport has that, too. On a Carnival cruise to Freeport, you can enjoy a sun-baked day of your own kind of fun. Cruise to Freeport, Bahamas, and make your island getaway fantasies come true.

Central America & Caribbean.name: George Town
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
George Town, the vibrant capital of the Cayman Islands, is situated on Grand Cayman's western coast. As the largest city in this British Overseas Territory, it serves as the epicenter of the nation's financial services industry, housing numerous banks and trust companies. 

 Beyond its corporate facade, George Town boasts a rich cultural tapestry, with bustling markets, historic landmarks, and pristine beaches like the renowned Seven Mile Beach. Visitors can immerse themselves in a blend of modern amenities and Caribbean charm, making it a must-visit destination.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Grand Cayman
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruise to Grand Cayman, go barefoot in the sand and be oh-so- elegant... all in one day. Enjoy laid-back British civility in a sun-splashed Caribbean tropical locale on your Carnival Grand Cayman cruise. Don’t let the island’s scrubland terrain fool you — a spectacular marine paradise lies just offshore, where a living undersea wall tapers off dramatically into the ocean depths. Cruises to Grand Cayman bring you directly to the best scuba diving and snorkeling in the kaleidoscopic Cayman Islands. When you get up close and personal with the resident stingrays and turtles, you’ll soon learn why beach-goers and nature lovers rank cruises to the Grand Cayman as some of the best in the Caribbean.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Grand Turk
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Let the name fool you — Grand Turk, of the Turks & Caicos islands, may be small but it’s packed with scenic punch and historic charm. Carnival cruises to Grand Turk deliver you to this enchanted island outpost dotted with old windmills, grassy trails and picture-perfect beaches. Discover an oasis of green set in aquamarine seas, ringed by a pristine coral reef and the steep wall of the continental shelf.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Grenada
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises to Grenada are ideal for those with a taste for nature and robust flavors. Known as the “Isle of Spice,” Grenada definitely leaves a flavor in your memories after a visit. There’s a reason for this, too… and it’s more than just the island’s role as one of the world’s most important producers of nutmeg, cinnamon, cloves, ginger and allspice. Meshed somewhere in the tropical air, the winding rivers and the fertile soil — a result of the island’s volcanic origins — is a life energy of a distinctly south Caribbean sort. On your Carnival cruise to Grenada, you’ll notice it at the beaches, hear it in the reggae and calypso, and taste it in rich local delicacies… like a dish known as “oildown” (breadfruit and fresh fish). Treat your palate — and all of your senses — to the magic of cruises to Grenada.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Guadeloupe
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Of all the Lesser Antiles, Guadeloupe is among the greatest… and it’s about as fun as it is French. And yes, it’s totally French. The island group also has the title of largest European Union territory in North America. (Now you know!) Guadeloupe’s size gives you many opportunities to explore and see why the island group is called “Pearl of the French Caribbean.” As the name might suggest, Grande-Terre is one of the biggest islands of the pearl, and Sainte-Anne is the most popular resort town you’ll find anywhere in Guadeloupe. Not far away you’ll find Fleur d ’Epée, a fort whose elevation provides superior views of the island… and some of its islandy neighbors. And for our final installment of Local Fun You Can’t Pronounce , Cascades des Ecrevisses in Guadeloupe National Park proves that a splashy natural wading pool — complete with waterfall — is actually a universal language.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Half Moon Cay
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
After you see the pristine beaches and breathtaking turquoise waters, it's no surprise to learn that Half Moon Cay was named Best Private Island. But it held the title for more than 20 years… and you don't earn that type of respect without having great ways for visitors to really make the most of paradise.

So grab a Carnival cruise to this exclusive destination, and from your first toe in the sand you’ll understand why it’s anything but typical. All the excitement aboard your Carnival cruise pauses for a bit, opening up your vacation to a private island experience.

Cozy up and lounge with your someone-special in a clam-shell, a shady hideaway for two. If you’re looking for more private stretch-out space there are cabanas and villas to rent. And who can resist the siren’s call of an expert massage by the beach?

You know, kicking back is encouraged at Half Moon Cay... but against this lush backdrop there’s still so much to do! Play in turquoise seas, ride horseback on powdery white sand, or gently paddle a kayak through acres of protected lagoon, encountering silky-smooth stingrays.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Havana
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Havana, Cuba’s vibrant capital, is a city of contrasts, blending rich history with lively modern culture. Known for its pastel-colored colonial buildings, vintage cars, and the infectious rhythm of salsa music, Havana offers an immersive experience for every visitor. The city is divided into neighborhoods, each with its unique character, from the historic streets of Old Havana to the coastal beauty of Vedado. Its lively streets, bustling markets, and scenic views of the Malecón seawall make Havana a captivating destination for history buffs and adventurers alike.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Jacksonville
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises from Jacksonville offer the perfect blend of beachfront and urban exploration. Get the best of a laid-back beach town and a buzzing city when you book a Carnival cruise from the beautiful port of Jacksonville, Florida. The Sunshine State’s largest city has a redeveloped and revitalized downtown, full of upbeat shopping and lively restaurants, bisected by the scenic St. Johns River and its Southbank Riverwalk. Jacksonville is a gateway to the islands of the Bahamas but you’ll also have 20 miles of sandy beach life and endless acres of wildlife parks to enjoy before you board your Carnival cruise from Jacksonville.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Jost Van Dyke
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Jost Van Dyke, a jewel of the British Virgin Islands, spans just over eight square kilometres but offers much more than its size suggests. This island is known for its serene beaches and is a favourite for those seeking a laid-back escape. 

 The main settlement, Great Harbour, is home to a small population and serves as the social hub, where locals and visitors gather at famous beach bars like Foxy’s. White Bay is another highlight, known for its soft, white sand and crystal-clear waters, ideal for snorkeling or simply relaxing.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Kingstown
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Kingstown, the capital of St. Vincent and the Grenadines, lies along the island’s southwestern coast, serving as the main port and economic hub for this island nation. Known as the “City of Arches” for its Georgian-style stone archways, Kingstown blends colonial charm with Caribbean vibrancy. 

 The city is bustling with markets, historic churches, and botanical gardens. Visitors will find themselves surrounded by stunning coastal views, friendly locals, and a lively downtown, making it the perfect gateway to explore both St. Vincent and the nearby Grenadine islands.

"Capital city" may bring to mind a bustling metropolis, but you'll find that it couldn't be further from the truth for Kingstown, on St. Vincent. "Laid-back port city" is more Kingstown's speed, and its London-style historical structures seem content to turn things back a century or two. The architecture has earned it the nickname "City of Arches" for the distinctive triangle shape you'll find on the façade of its typical Georgian-style buildings. The tropical climate makes St. Vincent hospitable for botanical gardens and plantations of fruits like nutmeg and breadfruit, grown in the island's rich volcanic soil. You can actually pick some up at Kingston's lively markets, in addition to the crafts and keepsakes, handmade jewelry and more treasures.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Kralendijk
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Kralendijk, the capital of Bonaire, is a charming Caribbean town known for its vibrant culture and historical significance. The name "Kralendijk" is derived from the Dutch word "Koralendijk," meaning "coral reef," reflecting the area's rich marine environment. The town features a blend of colorful Caribbean architecture and colonial Dutch influences, with landmarks like Fort Oranje, constructed in 1639, serving as a testament to its strategic importance during colonial times. Today, Kralendijk is a hub for tourism, offering visitors a mix of historical sites, shopping, and waterfront dining, all within a walkable and scenic setting.

Central America & Caribbean.name: La Paz
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Pull into the Sea of Cortez on Carnival cruises to La Paz, Mexico. The breezy capital of Baja California Sur sits on a peaceful bay of rocky islands and secluded sandy beaches. Just below the surface, spirited sea lions and gentle whale sharks swim alongside adventurous snorkelers. Discover the vibrant underwater life of what author Jacques Cousteau once labeled the “world’s aquarium” when you visit La Paz on your cruise to Mexico.

Central America & Caribbean.name: La Romana
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
La Romana, located on the southeastern coast of the Dominican Republic, is a vibrant city known for its pristine beaches, upscale resorts, and rich cultural heritage. Overlooking the Caribbean Sea and facing Catalina Island, it has evolved from a modest settlement into a bustling urban center. The city serves as a significant hub for tourism, industry, and commerce, contributing to its status as one of the country's wealthiest regions. Visitors can enjoy world-class golf courses, luxurious accommodations, and a variety of water-based activities, making La Romana a premier destination for travelers seeking both relaxation and adventure.

Whatever your vacation style — couple, family, big group — cruises to La Romana give you the chance to play on some of the Caribbean’s most beautiful beaches and lush terrain. This captivating slice of Dominican Republic tropics is found on the island’s southeast coast, and your Carnival cruise to La Romana takes you right there. Comb the cobblestone streets of a re-created medieval village, hunt for craft treasures, and soak in the pampered luxury of Casa de Campo, the 7,000-acre resort conveniently located next door. It’s this mix of culture, shopping and fun in the sun that makes cruises to the Dominican Republic — not to mention La Romana — some of the best bets in the Caribbean.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Limón Province
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Limón, located on Costa Rica's eastern Caribbean coast, serves as both a vital port city and the capital of Limón Province. Established in 1854, it has evolved into a bustling hub for commerce and tourism. The city's economy thrives on its port activities, facilitating the export of bananas and coffee, and welcoming numerous cruise ships annually. 

 Limón's rich cultural tapestry is influenced by Afro-Caribbean, Indigenous, and Mestizo communities, creating a vibrant atmosphere. Visitors can explore diverse attractions, from lush rainforests and pristine beaches to historical sites, all contributing to Limón's unique charm.

Cruise to Costa Rica to discover why the locals have been called the happiest people in the world. Costa Rica’s abundant ecotourism treasures await when you board your Carnival cruise to Limón. From here you can soar above rainforests, feel the mist from tumbling waterfalls, and swim in clear Caribbean seas. These make Limón, Costa Rica a top spot in the region for embarking on eco-adventures. When you cruise to Limón, don’t forget to check out the city itself, the source for millions of bananas that get shipped northward every year. Whether in the Caribbean waters or amongst nature on land, you’ll feel refreshed and invigorated by your cruise to Costa Rica.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Long Beach (Los Angeles)
Central America & Caribbean.overview: When we talk about cruising from Los Angeles, fyi: we’re talking about cruises from Long Beach. A short drive from LA is where you’ll find this seaside Southern California city that’s inseparable from its big-city neighbor, while undeniably moving to its own local beat. Dotted with dozens of parks, beaches and multiple public golf courses (fore times five!), Long Beach also boasts perhaps the world’s most unique hotel — located aboard the old-time ocean liner The Queen Mary (which is haunted, or so go the rumors). You can experience it before one of Carnival’s cruises from Long Beach, but definitely don’t miss the glitzy, big-city Los Angeles — the right place to experience the left-coast life. Take in the storied sights of this City of Angels, from its modern museums and high-end shopping to its funky seaside towns and star-studded SoCal sights dotting the hills and boulevards. Hollywood — hooray! So if you’re dreaming about a cruise from Los Angeles, remember: Carnival’s cruises from Long Beach are just the ticket.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Lucayan Knoll
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Freeport Lucayan, located on Grand Bahama Island, is a vibrant mix of beaches, cultural attractions, and luxury resorts. Known for its welcoming atmosphere, it provides a range of activities, from water sports and beach relaxation to shopping and dining at the Port Lucaya Marketplace. 

 The area is well-developed, with amenities catering to both tourists and locals. Grand Lucayan Resort offers an extensive experience with beautiful oceanfront settings, pools, and golf courses. With its stunning coastal views and variety of experiences, Freeport Lucayan is a top choice for those seeking both adventure and relaxation.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Mahogany Bay, Roatan
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Cruise to Mahogany Bay and you’ll feel like you won the island lottery. With silky-sand beaches, diamond-clear seas and 42 species of humming birds, the island of Roatan is truly a tropical paradise, one of the Caribbean’s hidden gems. And there’s no better way than a cruise to Honduras to experience this destination that blends great energy, island vibes and spectacular nature.

The largest of Honduras’s Bay Islands is a real beauty, built on a backbone of volcanic rock and ringed by the world’s second-largest coral reef. Carnival cruises to Roatan promise superb diving and fishing, plus totally swoon-worthy beaches. The lush landscape of the wildlife reserves and pristine marine parkland of Roatan awaits you on your cruise to Mahogany Bay.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Managua
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Managua, the capital and largest city of Nicaragua, is situated on the southwestern shore of Lake Managua. As the nation's political, economic, and cultural hub, it hosts key government institutions, vibrant markets, and a burgeoning arts scene. The city's layout is unique, with landmarks dispersed due to rebuilding efforts following past earthquakes. 

 Visitors can explore a mix of modern infrastructure and historical sites, including bustling plazas and serene lakeside promenades. Managua's warm climate and welcoming atmosphere make it an inviting destination for travelers seeking to experience Nicaragua's rich heritage and contemporary life.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Martinique
Central America & Caribbean.overview: You might guess from the name that Martinique has a little French in it. But you might be surprised to learn that this far-flung island is more than just a Francophile — it’s technically part of France. Pretty cool for a southern Caribbean gem, oui? The food consists not only of the French influences, but a hearty helping of the local staples like seafood and tropical fruits and vegetables. The big can’t-miss is a curry called “colombo,” the peak of local cuisine. The island’s actual peak comes in the form of Mont Pelée, standing 4,500 feet above sea level. There’s variety on this island, from forests to savannas to beaches. Speaking of beaches, you’ll find white-sand beaches on Martinique’s south end, with black-sand beaches to the north.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Mexico
Central America & Caribbean.overview: So close, yet so deliciously far. Mexico might be right down there, but it exists in another world entirely. Experience a whole enchilada’s worth of rich culture and fun on a cruise to Mexico. Each port — including favorites Cozumel, Cabo San Lucas and Puerto Vallarta — contributes its own distinct local flavor to the mix. Whether your Mexico cruise style is west-coast Riviera with its Pacific charm, east-coast Caribbean and its island mojo… or perhaps Baja and its out-of-this-world coastal scenery, you’ll find Mexico central to a great vacation.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Montego Bay
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Montego Bay, affectionately known as "MoBay," is Jamaica's second-largest city and a premier tourist destination. Nestled on the island's northwestern coast, it boasts pristine white-sand beaches, crystal-clear waters, and a vibrant cultural scene. 

 The city is renowned for its luxurious resorts, world-class golf courses, and bustling "Hip Strip" (Jimmy Cliff Boulevard), lined with restaurants, bars, and shops. Montego Bay serves as a gateway to the Caribbean, with the Sangster International Airport facilitating easy access for international travelers.

When it comes to cruises to Jamaica, this is a port you won’t want to miss. Prepare to let your hair down and kick back to seductive reggae rhythms on your cruise to Montego Bay. Boasting some of Jamaica’s finest beaches, shopping, restaurants and golf courses, “Mo Bay” (as it’s affectionately known) is a tropical live-wire. Are you ready to immerse yourself in the town’s festive energy? Carnival’s cruises to Montego Bay let you shop, swim, and play in Mo Bay to your heart’s content — and sample Jamaica’s rich culture on excursions into the island’s breathtaking countryside. History lovers can experience the grandeur of old-time mansions and golfers can test their skills on a course with a Caribbean backdrop. Whatever you’re into, we’ve got everybody covered on our cruises to Montego Bay.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Nassau
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Sightseeing, swimming and soaking up the island flavor — it's all yours for the taking on a Carnival cruise to Nassau, Bahamas. Boasting balmy breezes and jumping to a bouncy calypso beat, Nassau promises sizzling fun in the sun. The historic and cultural heart of The Bahamas is one of the world’s most popular cruise destinations — one million travelers board cruises to Nassau every year. Carnival guests come for fine beaches, shopping, water sports, and fun-filled attractions at the Atlantis Resort, connected to Nassau by a bridge to Paradise Island. Throw in a chance to swim with dolphins, or do a little slipping and sliding at a water park, and it's clear what makes Nassau a top spot for cruisers of all ages.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Norfolk
Central America & Caribbean.overview: If you live in the Mid-Atlantic area, might we suggest one of our cruises from Norfolk? Set sail, as thousands of seafaring Americans have, when you book a Carnival cruise from the 400-year-old seaport of Norfolk. The city is today the proud home of a sparkling waterfront filled with green parks, lively shopping malls, delicious restaurants… and even fascinating maritime museums and monuments. Before you cruise from Norfolk, you’ll find yourself near two colonial villages — Colonial Williamsburg and Olde Towne Portsmouth — plus miles of beaches, and a blooming botanical garden. So definitely make time to explore before your cruise from Virginia, and round out your vacation with some history and picturesque sightseeing. Cruises from Norfolk also come with serious seafaring credentials — the city is home to one of the best maritime academies in the country.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Ocho Rios
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Ocho Rios, often called "Ochi" by locals, is a picturesque coastal town on Jamaica's northern coast, known for its vibrant charm and natural beauty. Famous for its stunning beaches, cascading waterfalls, and lush greenery, this bustling town offers a perfect mix of relaxation and adventure. 

 Ocho Rios is a hub for tourists, featuring attractions like Dunn's River Falls, Mystic Mountain, and Dolphin Cove. Its vibrant markets, duty-free shops, and delectable Jamaican cuisine make it a must-visit destination for travelers seeking both serenity and excitement.

When it comes to dream vacations, an Ocho Rios cruise has to be near the top of the list. Nestled in an emerald Jamaican landscape, Ocho Rios is this island nation’s prime cruise destination. Your cruise to Ocho Rios docks near some of the Caribbean’s most fabled resorts and the cascading waterfalls of Dunn’s River. Enjoy the best of Jamaica on your Carnival cruise to Ocho Rios, whether you’re riding through lush sugarcane fields or touring the waterfront home where a legendary English novelist penned his early secret agent thrillers. From the reggae rhythm to the action-packed activities at Dunn’s River Falls, an Ocho Rios, Jamaica, cruise has all the elements of a blockbuster film — one where you’re the star. Start planning your Caribbean cruise with a call at Ocho Rios today and get ready for your close-up.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Oranjestad
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Oranjestad, Aruba’s vibrant capital, is a blend of colorful Dutch colonial architecture, scenic beaches, and lively markets. Known for its friendly locals and walkable layout, the city invites visitors to explore its mix of modern boutiques, historic landmarks, and Caribbean charm. 

 Located along Aruba’s western coast, Oranjestad offers easy access to beautiful waterfronts, cultural sites, and unique shopping spots. With its year-round sunny climate and bustling attractions, Oranjestad is a must-see destination that captures the essence of Aruba’s warm, welcoming spirit.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Panama Canal
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
If you zoom your map way out, Panama looks like a small sliver of land. But cruises to the Panama Canal give us the chance for a zoomed-in, real-life look at perhaps last century’s greatest engineering achievement… along with its verdant neighboring lands. This testament to human audacity joins the Pacific and Caribbean in the most direct way possible, and is worth making a part of your vacation. Plus, when you're in Colón, that's your chance to check out the famous canal up-close, on a shore excursion! Panama Canal cruises also visit Central American ports like Limón and Cartagena… and even Caribbean island hotspots on the way. You’ll get to explore the local beaches and culture while heading to, or from, one of the greatest man-made marvels in the world. Panama Canal cruises promise one legendary trip. 

 Tip: On a few special, select cruises, you can even traverse the entire Panama Canal, going from Pacific to the Caribbean (or vice versa), a unique journey that takes over 20 hours!

Central America & Caribbean.name: Panama City
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Panama City, the capital of Panama, is a vibrant metropolis that seamlessly blends modernity with rich historical heritage. Situated at the Pacific entrance of the Panama Canal, the city serves as a pivotal hub for international trade and finance. 

 Its skyline is adorned with towering skyscrapers, while the historic district, Casco Viejo, showcases colonial-era architecture and cobblestone streets. The city's unique position between the Pacific Ocean and tropical rainforests offers visitors a diverse array of experiences, from bustling urban life to serene natural landscapes.

Zoom your map way out and Panama looks like a small sliver of land, so a trip through its famous canal is a quickie, right? But not so fast — traversing the Panama Canal actually takes at least 20 hours! Cruise it and enjoy a zoomed-in real-life look at perhaps last century’s greatest engineering achievement… along with its surrounding lush, verdant lands. This testament to human audacity joins the Pacific and Caribbean in the most direct way possible, and is worth making a leisurely part of your vacation.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Philipsburg
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Philipsburg, the capital of Dutch Sint Maarten, offers a perfect blend of vibrant culture, beautiful beaches, and exciting shopping experiences. Nestled between Great Bay and the Salt Pond, this lively town boasts stunning coastal views and historical attractions. Tourists can explore Front Street, famous for its duty-free shopping, where luxury goods and local crafts can be found. 

 The Boardwalk is a must-visit for enjoying waterfront restaurants and lively beach bars. History enthusiasts will love Fort Amsterdam, which offers insights into the island’s colonial past. With its mix of natural beauty and lively atmosphere, Philipsburg is a must-see Caribbean destination.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Playa del Carmen
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Playa del Carmen, nestled along Mexico’s Riviera Maya, is a vibrant coastal city renowned for its pristine beaches, turquoise waters, and lively atmosphere. Once a quaint fishing village, it has transformed into a top destination offering luxury resorts, boutique hotels, and bustling nightlife. The city’s iconic Fifth Avenue, or "Quinta Avenida," serves as the heart of activity, lined with shops, restaurants, and entertainment venues. Whether you seek relaxation by the sea or an energetic urban vibe, Playa del Carmen offers an unforgettable experience for every traveler.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Port Elizabeth
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Bequia, a gem in the Grenadines, is known for its tranquil beaches, friendly locals, and vibrant maritime culture. The island’s capital, Port Elizabeth, nestled along the shores of Admiralty Bay, serves as the bustling heart of Bequia, offering visitors a range of experiences from yachting to exploring local markets. 

 The Belmont Walkway, a scenic coastal path, connects Port Elizabeth to nearby beaches and restaurants, making it perfect for a leisurely stroll. With its mix of modern conveniences and rich cultural heritage, Port Elizabeth offers an authentic Caribbean island experience.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Port of Spain
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Port of Spain, the vibrant capital of Trinidad and Tobago, is a bustling metropolis located on Trinidad's northwest coast along the Gulf of Paria. As the nation's administrative and commercial hub, it boasts a dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers, colonial architecture, and cultural landmarks. 

 The city is renowned for its lively festivals, particularly the annual Carnival, which showcases its rich musical heritage, including calypso and soca. Visitors can explore diverse neighborhoods, savor local cuisine, and experience the warmth and hospitality of its multicultural population.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Princess Cays
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruise to Princess Cays and you’ll come ashore in Love Boat Marina — a sign that this private resort on the island of Eleuthera, Bahamas was designed to be classic cruising perfection. Make landfall and discover an island’s worth of vacation fun and amenities. You’ll find a BBQ pit already fired up for lunch, and a supervised kids’ area ready to receive the little ones. Somewhere among the palm trees you might spy the exclusive adult-only retreat. By now it’s clear that Princess Cays is good for more than just a beach day, but plan on fitting one in anyway — this wouldn’t be a prime cruise destination without great things to do, and great ways to get wet, like swimming, kayaking and sailing.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Progreso, Yucatán
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruise to the Yucatán Peninsula for an unbeatable combination of breathtaking beaches and fascinating history. Carnival cruises to Progreso dock on Mexico’s Emerald Coast alongside the gulf, where hot pink flamingos gather by the hundreds and sun-bleached Maya pyramids rise from the jungle. This explosion of color and cultural landmarks is yours to explore on your cruise to Yucatán. Inland from Progreso’s golden beaches are the spectacular Maya ruins of Uxmal and the colorful city of Mérida, the region’s best shopping stop for handmade crafts. Discover the Maya world’s natural beauty and ancient history with cruises to Progreso, Mexico.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Puntarenas Province
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Puntarenas, located along Costa Rica's Pacific coastline, is a vibrant city celebrated for its rich cultural heritage and picturesque seaside charm. Known as the "Pearl of the Pacific," it serves as a gateway to the country's stunning beaches and national parks. 

 Visitors can stroll along the scenic Paseo de los Turistas, a promenade lined with local eateries, souvenir shops, and stunning ocean views. Puntarenas offers a blend of laid-back coastal living and lively urban energy, making it an ideal destination for those seeking relaxation and adventure.

Bordering a gentle stretch of sand kissed by Pacific breezes, the former rough-and-tumble port town of Puntarenas, Costa Rica is now a laid-back seaside retreat. With a Puntarenas cruise, you can stroll the mile-long seafront Paseo de los Turistas boardwalk past restaurants, cafés and colorful souvenir stands. On your Carnival cruise to Puntarenas, relax on the powdery beach and watch the sun melt into the Gulf of Nicoya.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Road Harbour
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Tortola, the largest of the British Virgin Islands, is a captivating blend of stunning natural beauty and Caribbean charm. The island is known for its lush green hills, crystal-clear waters, and white sandy beaches, making it a haven for beach lovers and water sports enthusiasts. 

 Its capital, Road Town, offers a laid-back atmosphere with vibrant local markets, boutique shops, and colorful colonial architecture. Whether you’re exploring hidden coves or relaxing at a beachside café, Tortola offers a tranquil escape paired with the beauty of the Caribbean.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Roseau
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Roseau, Dominica’s vibrant capital, is nestled between lush mountains and the Caribbean Sea, offering a unique mix of natural beauty and urban charm. Known for its colorful French-Creole architecture and bustling market, Roseau provides a lively atmosphere where visitors can enjoy local culture, cuisine, and scenic views. 

 The city’s compact layout makes it easy to explore on foot, with botanical gardens, waterfront spots, and cultural sites nearby. Roseau’s warm, welcoming vibe and rich Caribbean influence make it an inviting gateway to Dominica’s unspoiled landscapes.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Saint George
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
St. George's, the vibrant capital of Grenada, is renowned for its picturesque horseshoe-shaped harbor, colorful Georgian architecture, and lively markets. Nestled between lush hills and the Caribbean Sea, this charming city offers visitors a blend of natural beauty and cultural richness. The bustling Carenage waterfront is lined with shops, cafes, and historic buildings, providing a perfect setting for leisurely strolls. 

 Nearby, the famous Grand Anse Beach invites sun-seekers to its pristine sands and turquoise waters. St. George's seamlessly combines the warmth of its people with the allure of its scenic landscapes, making it a must-visit destination.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Saint Thomas
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
St. Thomas, part of the U.S. Virgin Islands, is a tropical paradise known for its crystal-clear waters, lush landscapes, and rich culture. With a bustling capital in Charlotte Amalie, the island offers visitors a perfect blend of relaxation and adventure. 

 Tourists flock here for the beautiful beaches, duty-free shopping, and world-class water activities like snorkeling and sailing. The island’s vibrant nightlife, waterfront restaurants, and welcoming locals make it an ideal destination for couples, families, and solo travelers seeking a Caribbean escape.

Cruises that visit St. Thomas offer some of the best photo opportunities in the Caribbean. That’s why a Carnival cruise to the Virgin Islands tops many folk’s vacation wish lists. Seafarers from around the globe have flocked to the sheltered harbors of St. Thomas since pirates sailed these seas. Today, cruises to St. Thomas dock in one of the busiest and most developed ports in the West Indies: Charlotte Amalie, the island’s historic capital. Immerse yourself in the rhythm of the market bustle, and swing to the beat of scratch bands. Or plant yourself on a beautiful beach and relax the day away. The choice is yours when you cruise to St. Thomas in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

Central America & Caribbean.name: San José Province
Central America & Caribbean.overview: San José, the vibrant capital of Costa Rica, is nestled in the heart of the Central Valley at an elevation of approximately 1,160 meters (3,800 feet) above sea level. As the nation's largest city, it serves as the political, economic, and cultural hub, boasting a population of around 352,381 residents as of 2022. The city seamlessly blends historical charm with modern urban life, featuring world-class museums, theaters, and parks. Its central location makes San José an ideal base for exploring Costa Rica's diverse landscapes, from lush rainforests to pristine beaches.

Central America & Caribbean.name: San Juan
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises from San Juan, Puerto Rico offer a magical blend of natural island charm and man-made wonders. Historic San Juan, once nothing more than cobblestone streets and the distinctive architecture of the Spanish who founded it, is now a modern world capital. And, as you’ll see on Carnival’s cruises from Puerto Rico, it’s kept these classical features! Among the peaceful shaded courtyards and commanding colonial fortresses of El Morro and San Cristobal, urban explorers will find a cosmopolitan city of wide boulevards, lively cafés, highly-regarded galleries and designer boutiques… not to mention gold-sand beaches and nearby rainforest jungles. San Juan, Puerto Rico is where the old world meets the new — and you can enjoy them both when you choose one of our cruises from San Juan.

Central America & Caribbean.name: San Miguel de Cozumel
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
San Miguel de Cozumel, the island's largest city, serves as the vibrant heart of Cozumel, Mexico's largest inhabited island. Situated on the western coast, this bustling metropolis seamlessly blends modern amenities with rich cultural heritage. 

 Visitors can explore colorful streets lined with shops, restaurants, and historic landmarks, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Caribbean Sea. The city's central square, Plaza del Sol, acts as a focal point for social and cultural activities, offering a glimpse into the daily life of its residents.

Cozumel is proof that Mexico can do ‘island flavor’ with the best of the Caribbean. It may not be the biggest island in the sea, but Cozumel offers a huge variety of things to do, see, taste and explore. This island just happens to be perched atop a coral reef, which promises hours of snorkeling or scuba diving fun. Cruises to Cozumel dock at the island’s heart — just minutes from San Miguel’s seafront shops and the white sand beach at Chankanaab National Park. Drift among star corals and sea fans on a Cozumel diving excursion or simply kick back on the beach beneath your own palm palapa as the turquoise waves roll in. With non-stop zip line action, ancient discoveries at the nearby Mayan ruins, or time spent with your toes in the sand, a cruise to Cozumel may just top your best vacations list… before you even get home.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Santo Domingo
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Santo Domingo, the bustling capital of the Dominican Republic, is a vibrant city where history, culture, and modern life seamlessly blend. Located on the southern coast, it’s the largest city in the Caribbean, offering visitors a dynamic mix of colonial charm, sandy beaches, and lively nightlife. 

 Known for its friendly locals, Santo Domingo invites tourists to explore its lively markets, world-class restaurants, and diverse neighborhoods. From its bustling Malecón waterfront to the tranquil beauty of the Colonial Zone, Santo Domingo offers something for everyone to enjoy.

It was rumoured to be the the inspiration for the musical South Pacific, journey to the tropical paradise of Espiritu Santo, or Santo for short.
 On a Santo cruise you can experience everything there is to love about the Vanuatu archipelago, all in one spot. With a Santo cruise you can dive among the wrecks of the dumped American naval and military craft at Million Dollar Point, swim the coral reef in crystal clear waters, walk along the white beaches, and experience the relaxation of taking a dip in the Blue Hole. And do it all onboard Carnival, creating fun and adventure along the way.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Scarborough
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Scarborough, the lively capital of Tobago, is a bustling port town on the island’s southwestern coast. With around 17,000 residents, it serves as the island’s main economic and administrative center, blending a rich cultural atmosphere with the island's natural beauty. 

 This hilly city, centered around Scarborough Harbour, offers stunning Atlantic Ocean views and is the primary entry point for visitors arriving by cruise ship or ferry. Attractions like the bustling Scarborough Market and scenic Botanical Gardens highlight the town’s vibrant, community-centered feel, welcoming tourists with a taste of local life.

Central America & Caribbean.name: St Barths
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Gustavia, the vibrant capital of St. Barthélemy (St. Barths), is known for its picturesque harbor, luxurious atmosphere, and charming colonial architecture. Originally built around a natural harbor, Gustavia has transformed into a hub for high-end tourism, featuring upscale boutiques, fine dining, and stunning beach views. 

 The city's downtown area showcases pastel-colored buildings and red-roofed structures reflecting its French-Caribbean flair. Beyond its sophisticated ambiance, Gustavia offers scenic coastal roads, perfect for leisurely walks and spotting luxury yachts that often dock at its lively marina.

Central America & Caribbean.name: St. Croix
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises to St Croix offer some of the most striking scenery you can see on a cruise — on land, on the water, and beneath it. The island also has a fascinating history. St. Croix’s path to becoming a U.S. Virgin Island took it through the hands of France and Denmark. While we’re talking geography, cruises to St. Croix let you explore a variety of it, ranging from the east end’s desert vistas to rainforest and palm trees on the west. Underwater, coral reefs are abundant, making SCUBA diving a great way to see best-hidden local sights during cruises to St Croix. As far as unwinding goes, there’s rum — the good stuff — and plenty of it, with famous brands’ distilleries located right here.

Central America & Caribbean.name: St. Kitts
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Cruises to St. Kitts are a nature-lover’s dream come true, and will wow anyone looking for a laidback Caribbean vacation full of postcard-ready scenery. Rainforests, waterfalls, and lovely crescents of sand aren’t far from the ship on your Carnival cruise to St. Kitts. Cane fields climb the slopes of volcanic peaks, and the ruins of old mills and colonial plantation houses blanket the island. As you’ll find on St. Kitts, there is plenty of action in the form of zip-lining and hiking but at heart, St. Kitts remains a sleepy and charming tropical community. Cruise to St. Kitts to bask in the Caribbean’s sunny geniality and natural splendor.

Central America & Caribbean.name: St. Lucia
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Take a walk through St. Lucia’s landscapes of magenta bougainvillea tumbling from mossy peaks, and listen to birdsong filling the air. This is just a slice of island life that awaits on cruises to St. Lucia. With some of the Caribbean’s most dramatic scenery — from the velvety twin peaks of the Pitons to the shimmering white-sand beaches — independent St. Lucia speaks with a decidedly French lilt. Climb green-mantled mountains and snorkel in jade seas on your sun-filled Carnival cruise to St. Lucia.

Central America & Caribbean.name: St. Maarten
Central America & Caribbean.overview: Explore two colorful nations on one exotic island on one vacation. Split down the middle centuries ago by two European powers, this 37-mile isle offers cosmopolitan culture, international shopping, and world-class cuisine with a laid-back Caribbean atmosphere. Cruise to St. Maarten and you’ll find the island’s natural attributes are equally fine: the rugged coastline shelters silky crescents of sand framed by green undulating hills. Enjoy the laidback vibe of the beach or put on your hiking shoes and explore ruins of historic forts. One thing’s for sure — when you cruise to St. Maarten you’ll come home with amazing memories that will last a lifetime.

Central America & Caribbean.name: The Bahamas
Central America & Caribbean.overview: You know the name, the laid-back attitude and where to find them, but you’ll just have to visit The Bahamas to truly appreciate this classic cruise destination in the blue Atlantic, where the living’s easy. And it’s not bad on a Bahamas cruise either! The central port of Nassau is the bustling capital of the country — relatively bustling, of course — while Freeport is all chill, all the time. And then there’s spots like Celebration Key ™ , RelaxAway, Half Moon Cay ™ and Princess Cays: exclusive-destination paradise. Enjoy sailing, snorkeling, strolling pearly pink-sand beaches, and swimming through crystalline turquoise seas on a Bahamas cruise.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Tortola
Central America & Caribbean.overview: If you’re looking for some of the most scenic blue ocean vistas in the Caribbean, you’ll find them on cruises to the British Virgin Islands. How many other islands give you the chance to sail on channel-crossing yachts destined for the Norman, Cooper, Salt or Pepper Islands? On cruises to Tortola, you can reach all of these, or jump right in and swim or snorkel into underwater caves and get up-close with colorful coral reefs. Another highlight of cruises to the British Virgin Islands is a scenic island tour that takes you up Fort Hill to Cane Garden Bay, where you can relax on spectacular white-sand beaches. Enjoy the tales of this historic waterway as you retrace the paths of Christopher Columbus and Sir Francis Drake on Carnival’s cruises to Tortola.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Virgin Gorda
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Virgin Gorda, the third-largest island in the British Virgin Islands, is famed for its unique natural landscape and tranquil ambiance. Known for stunning beaches, massive granite rock formations, and warm Caribbean waters, the island provides both seclusion and adventure. 

 A highlight is The Baths, where huge boulders create natural pools and hidden caves, ideal for exploration. Virgin Gorda maintains a relaxed, welcoming atmosphere, largely untouched by heavy tourism. Visitors can enjoy breathtaking views, unspoiled beaches, and charming island villages, making it a paradise for nature lovers and beachgoers.

Central America & Caribbean.name: Willemstad
Central America & Caribbean.overview:
Willemstad, the vibrant capital of Curaçao, is renowned for its colorful Dutch colonial architecture and rich cultural tapestry. Situated on the island's southern coast, this UNESCO World Heritage Site is divided by the Sint Anna Bay into four historic districts: Punda, Otrobanda, Pietermaai, and Scharloo. 

 Visitors can explore lively markets, pristine beaches, and a diverse culinary scene that reflects the island's multicultural heritage. The city's unique blend of European and Caribbean influences offers a captivating experience for travelers seeking both history and modern attractions.

Eastern Europe.name: Budapest
Eastern Europe.overview: Budapest, the enchanting capital of Hungary, is renowned for its striking architecture, vibrant cultural scene, and therapeutic thermal baths. Straddling the Danube River, the city is divided into two contrasting halves: Buda, with its hilly terrain and historic landmarks, and Pest, the bustling flatlands housing lively markets and iconic attractions. Known as the "Paris of the East," Budapest offers a perfect blend of old-world charm and modern vibrancy. Its scenic riverbanks, listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, make it a favorite destination for travelers worldwide.

Eastern Europe.name: Donostia-San Sebastian
Eastern Europe.overview: Donostia-San Sebastián, often simply called San Sebastián, is a vibrant coastal city in Spain's Basque Country. Renowned for its stunning beaches, such as La Concha and Zurriola, the city offers a harmonious blend of natural beauty and urban sophistication. Its picturesque Old Town, or Parte Vieja, is a maze of narrow streets lined with traditional pintxos bars, inviting visitors to indulge in the local culinary delights. The city's commitment to culture is evident in its numerous festivals, museums, and theaters, making it a year-round destination for travelers seeking both relaxation and enrichment.

Eastern Europe.name: Kuşadası
Eastern Europe.overview: Kuşadası, meaning "Bird Island" in Turkish, is a vibrant resort town on Turkey's Aegean coast. Located approximately 95 kilometers south of İzmir, it serves as a gateway to the ancient city of Ephesus. The town is renowned for its turquoise waters, sandy beaches, and a large marina accommodating up to 600 boats. Kuşadası's lively atmosphere is complemented by a variety of restaurants, shops, and a bustling nightlife, making it a popular destination for tourists seeking both relaxation and cultural exploration.

Eastern Europe.name: Moscow
Eastern Europe.overview: Moscow, the vibrant capital of Russia, seamlessly blends modern sophistication with centuries-old traditions. As the political, cultural, and economic hub of the nation, the city is renowned for its iconic landmarks, bustling streets, and thriving arts scene. The Moscow River meanders through its heart, adding charm to its skyline dominated by historical domes and towering skyscrapers. From the world-famous Red Square to its expansive green parks and dynamic nightlife, Moscow offers a captivating mix of experiences, making it a must-visit destination for travelers.

Eastern Europe.name: Prague
Eastern Europe.overview: Prague, the capital and largest city of the Czech Republic, is situated on the Vltava River and is home to approximately 1.4 million people. Renowned for its well-preserved medieval architecture, the city boasts a harmonious blend of Romanesque, Gothic, Renaissance, and Baroque styles. Landmarks such as the Charles Bridge, Old Town Square, and Prague Castle contribute to its picturesque skyline. As a political, cultural, and economic hub of Central Europe, Prague offers a vibrant arts scene, numerous museums, and a rich tapestry of historical sites, making it a must-visit destination for travelers seeking both beauty and history.

Eastern Europe.name: Saint Petersburg
Eastern Europe.overview: St. Petersburg, Russia’s cultural capital, is a city of grandeur and history located along the Neva River. Renowned for its opulent palaces, vibrant art scene, and intricate canals, it seamlessly blends imperial elegance with modern vibrancy. Often called the "Venice of the North," the city is a hub for world-class museums, theaters, and galleries. Its architecture, from Baroque to Neoclassical styles, showcases centuries of craftsmanship. With its lively nightlife, fine dining, and unique charm, St. Petersburg offers an unforgettable experience for visitors seeking cultural and historical immersion.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Cairo
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Cairo, the sprawling capital of Egypt, is a vibrant city that effortlessly blends ancient history with modern life. Situated along the Nile River, it is known for its iconic landmarks, bustling streets, and rich cultural heritage. As one of the largest cities in Africa and the Middle East, Cairo offers a dynamic urban experience, from its lively markets and world-class museums to its contemporary dining and entertainment scenes. The city's warm hospitality and enduring charm make it a must-visit destination for travelers seeking a mix of the old and new.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Casablanca
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Casablanca, Morocco's largest city, serves as the nation's economic and business hub. Situated on the Atlantic coast, it boasts a population exceeding 3.7 million residents. The city's skyline is a blend of modern infrastructure and historic architecture, reflecting its rich cultural tapestry. Casablanca's bustling port is among Africa's largest, underscoring its pivotal role in trade and commerce. Visitors can explore a variety of attractions, from vibrant markets and art deco buildings to the impressive Hassan II Mosque, which stands as a testament to the city's architectural prowess.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Doha
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Doha, the capital city of Qatar, is a vibrant metropolis situated on the east coast of the Qatar Peninsula along the Persian Gulf. As the nation's political and economic hub, it is home to over 80% of Qatar's population, reflecting its status as the fastest-growing city in the country. The cityscape is a blend of modern skyscrapers and traditional Islamic architecture, showcasing landmarks like the Museum of Islamic Art and the futuristic skyline of the West Bay district. Doha's rich cultural heritage, coupled with its rapid modernization, offers tourists a unique experience that seamlessly merges tradition with innovation.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Dubai
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Dubai, the most populous city in the United Arab Emirates, is renowned for its rapid transformation from a modest fishing village to a global metropolis. Strategically located on the southeast coast of the Persian Gulf, it serves as a pivotal financial and trading hub, connecting markets worldwide. The city's skyline is adorned with architectural marvels, including the iconic Burj Khalifa, the world's tallest building. Beyond its modern infrastructure, Dubai offers pristine beaches, luxury shopping destinations, and a rich cultural tapestry, making it a premier destination for travelers seeking both adventure and relaxation.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Haifa
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Haifa, Israel’s third-largest city, is a vibrant metropolis blending modernity with natural beauty. Nestled on the slopes of Mount Carmel overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, it is a hub for culture, innovation, and scenic landscapes. Known for its harmonious coexistence of diverse religions and cultures, Haifa offers visitors a welcoming and inclusive atmosphere. The city is famed for its stunning Baháʼí Gardens, bustling port, and pristine beaches. Haifa also serves as a gateway to northern Israel’s attractions, making it an essential destination for travelers.

Middle East & North Africa.name: İstanbul
Middle East & North Africa.overview:
Istanbul, the cultural and economic heart of Turkey, is a vibrant metropolis that straddles two continents, Europe and Asia, and is divided by the shimmering Bosphorus Strait. As the only city in the world bridging two continents, Istanbul offers a unique blend of ancient history and modern dynamism. The city’s skyline is a stunning mix of Ottoman mosques, Byzantine churches, and sleek skyscrapers, reflecting its multicultural heritage. Bustling bazaars, such as the Grand Bazaar, coexist with contemporary neighborhoods and trendy cafes. From its iconic landmarks to its lively streets, Istanbul mesmerizes travelers with its timeless charm.

Sail up the Bosphorus Strait… with Europe on one side of the ship and Asia on the other! Choose sides as you follow in the footsteps of Byzantine emperors, Ottoman sultans, and modern fashionistas on your cruise to Istanbul. This is Turkey’s commercial capital, and its glittering palaces and mosaic-filled mosques line the shores, welcoming you with a cheery “merhaba” as you prepare to explore a city where carpet dealers still haggle at the 500-year-old Grand Bazaar, and rooftop bars serve creative cocktails to funky DJ beats. Check out the upside-down Medusa head sculptures at the Underground Cistern, duck into a Turkish tavern for a meal on Nevizade Street, or explore ornate mosques.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Jerusalem
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Jerusalem, the capital city of Israel, is a unique blend of ancient history and modern urban life. Situated in the Judean Mountains, it holds profound significance for Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. The city is divided into the Old City, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the New City, which features contemporary neighborhoods and bustling markets. With a population of approximately 971,800 residents, Jerusalem is a melting pot of cultures and traditions. Visitors can explore sacred sites, vibrant markets, and diverse culinary scenes, all within a city that seamlessly integrates its rich past with a dynamic present.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Marrakesh
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Marrakech, known as the “Red City” for its terracotta-hued buildings, is a vibrant destination that beautifully blends tradition with modernity. Nestled at the foothills of the Atlas Mountains, this Moroccan gem is famed for its bustling souks, exquisite gardens, and captivating palaces. The city is a sensory wonderland, offering colorful markets, aromatic spices, and an energetic medina teeming with life. Whether exploring its historic landmarks or indulging in its world-class dining, Marrakech promises an unforgettable experience for every traveler.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Muscat
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Muscat, the capital city of Oman, is a harmonious blend of tradition and modernity, nestled between the rugged Hajar Mountains and the serene Gulf of Oman. As the largest city in the country, it serves as the political, economic, and cultural heart of the nation. The city's architecture reflects its rich heritage, with white-washed buildings, grand mosques, and traditional souks juxtaposed against contemporary structures. Muscat's well-developed infrastructure includes modern amenities, extensive public transportation, and the Muscat International Airport, facilitating easy access for travelers. The city's coastal location offers picturesque beaches and a bustling port, enhancing its appeal to tourists seeking both relaxation and cultural immersion.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Nairobi
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Nairobi, the capital and largest city of Kenya, is a vibrant metropolis blending urban sophistication with natural beauty. Situated at an elevation of approximately 1,795 meters (5,889 feet), it enjoys a temperate climate year-round. The city's name derives from the Maasai phrase "Enkare Nairobi," meaning "place of cool waters," referencing the Nairobi River that flows through it. As a regional hub, Nairobi hosts numerous international organizations, including the United Nations Environment Programme. Its dynamic culture, diverse population, and proximity to wildlife make it a compelling destination for tourists seeking both adventure and urban experiences.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Sharm El-Sheikh
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Sharm el-Sheikh, often referred to as the "Jewel of the Red Sea," is a world-renowned resort city located on the southern tip of Egypt’s Sinai Peninsula. Known for its crystal-clear waters, vibrant coral reefs, and golden sandy beaches, it is a paradise for divers and sun-seekers alike. The city boasts a lively atmosphere with luxury resorts, bustling bazaars, and vibrant nightlife. Sharm el-Sheikh’s strategic location between Africa and Asia also makes it a gateway to unique desert adventures and stunning marine biodiversity.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Sinai Peninsula
Middle East & North Africa.overview: The Sinai Peninsula, a vast desert expanse surrounded by the Red Sea and Mediterranean, offers an intriguing blend of natural beauty and cultural significance. Renowned for its rugged mountains, pristine beaches, and vibrant coral reefs, the region is a haven for adventure enthusiasts and nature lovers alike. Its coastal resorts, such as Sharm El Sheikh and Dahab, are celebrated for their world-class diving and luxurious amenities. Beyond its picturesque landscapes, Sinai serves as a cultural crossroads, bridging Africa and Asia with its unique heritage and traditions.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Tel Aviv-Yafo
Middle East & North Africa.overview: Tel Aviv-Yafo, often simply referred to as Tel Aviv, is a bustling city located along Israel's Mediterranean coast. Known for its modern skyline, vibrant culture, and welcoming atmosphere, Tel Aviv offers a unique blend of innovation and tradition. The city is famous for its beaches, thriving nightlife, and culinary scene, with a wide array of restaurants, cafes, and bars. Visitors can explore art galleries, museums, and historical sites, while also enjoying the city's cosmopolitan vibe. Tel Aviv is a hub for technology, business, and culture, attracting tourists and professionals alike.

Middle East & North Africa.name: Tripoli
