In a modern, cosmopolitan city like Reykjavik, with some of the most sophisticated technology in the world, it’s easy to forget there’s serious history afoot, so check out the stories of the Vikings who once — like you — sailed Iceland, at one of the city’s many museums.

Northern Europe.name: Riga
Northern Europe.overview: Riga, the capital of Latvia, is a vibrant city located along the Daugava River, where history and modernity seamlessly blend. As the largest city in the Baltics, Riga is renowned for its stunning Art Nouveau architecture and UNESCO-listed Old Town. The cityscape is adorned with cobblestone streets, picturesque squares, and towering spires that reflect its rich cultural heritage. A thriving hub of commerce, culture, and cuisine, Riga offers an array of museums, cafes, and green spaces, ensuring a memorable experience for every visitor exploring this Baltic gem.

Northern Europe.name: St. Anthony, NL
Northern Europe.overview: Here’s a Newfoundland travel tip: DO visit St. Anthony, but DON’T miss the whale-watching. Got all that? After all, there’s a reason these incredible creatures are among the top draws to this town, which you’ll find situated at the tip of the Great Northern Peninsula. On land there’s even more wildlife that brings in the people and keeps their cameras busy… we’re talking ones like polar bears and the largest moose population almost anywhere. But let’s also talk humans for a second: there used to be real Vikings here, hundreds of years before European explorers got around to “discovering” the continent. Today, you can see how the Vikings lived at reconstructed sod-construction houses at the 11th-century village of L’Anse aux Meadows, not far from St. Anthony. Speaking of legendary, you’ll want to find out why the 19th-century Dr. Grenfell continues to be remembered as such an important figure among the local community.

Northern Europe.name: Stockholm
Northern Europe.overview:
Stockholm, the capital of Sweden, is a vibrant and picturesque city spread across 14 islands connected by over 50 bridges. Known as the "Venice of the North," it boasts stunning waterfront views, charming cobblestone streets, and a harmonious blend of historic and modern architecture. The city is a cultural hub, home to world-class museums, innovative design, and a thriving culinary scene. Surrounded by the tranquil waters of the Baltic Sea, Stockholm offers a unique mix of urban sophistication and natural beauty, making it an unforgettable destination for visitors.

Located about 45 minutes from the quaint port town of Nynäshamn, where cruise ships dock, Sweden’s famous Stockholm was founded on Stadsholmen, a Swedish island. Oh, and it’s still there — but if you check a map you’ll see there’s a bit more Stockholm to go around these days. The city has actually expanded outward, now covering a very impressive (checking our map…) 14 islands ! But great stats are just the beginning of what’s cool about Stockholm. This unique geography is predictably perfect for bridge lovers and canal explorers alike, while over on solid ground you’ll find equally cool sights to take in. Some options? Museums, palaces and old-town architecture made up of a delightful smörgåsbord of towers, cathedrals and copper-roofed buildings of all kinds. Plus there’s parkland galore, which accounts for a full 30% of Stockholm’s square meterage.

Northern Europe.name: Tallinn
Northern Europe.overview:
Tallinn, the charming capital of Estonia, blends medieval allure with modern vibrancy, making it a must-visit destination. Nestled on the Baltic Sea’s shores, this compact city boasts cobblestone streets, colorful facades, and a skyline dominated by ancient spires. Tallinn’s Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a beautifully preserved medieval masterpiece filled with lively cafes, boutique shops, and historic landmarks. Beyond its historical core, Tallinn offers contemporary art galleries, tech hubs, and a thriving culinary scene, creating a harmonious fusion of old and new.

Contemporary chic meets medieval charm in Tallinn, Estonia, located on the Baltic Sea. When you take a Carnival cruise to Tallinn, you’ll discover a vibrant capital city full of rich culture, hidden history, and picture-perfect streets. The city has preserved its limestone city walls that encircle the Old Town and 14th-century churches. As you wander through the town, you’ll see why Tallinn is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. While here, you can experience the city’s Gothic Town Hall Square, see the war monument at Freedom Square and visit the Rotermann Quarter.

Oceania.name: Adelaide
Oceania.overview:
Adelaide, the capital of South Australia, is a vibrant city known for its rich cultural scene, picturesque parks, and renowned festivals. Nestled between the rolling Adelaide Hills and the serene coastline, it offers a perfect blend of urban sophistication and natural beauty. Visitors can explore its bustling markets, world-class wineries, and historic architecture. The city is a gateway to the famous Barossa Valley wine region and Kangaroo Island, making it an ideal destination for adventure and relaxation alike. Adelaide’s charm lies in its welcoming vibe and diverse attractions.

Surrounded by rolling hills and stunning coastline, Adelaide is a friendly and vibrant city with a laidback, unhurried lifestyle. From bohemian laneways and alleys bursting with cafes and galleries to 19th-century sandstone architecture, you’re bound to fall in love with Adelaide as soon as you arrive.

Foodies, in particular, will love Central Market, dating back to 1869 and home to over 80 food stalls. Wine connoisseurs can enjoy a glass of the good stuff on a tour of Australia’s premier wine region, the Barossa Valley. Whether you want to learn about Indigenous heritage at the Australian Aboriginal Cultures Gallery, meet the animals at Adelaide Zoo, or take your time exploring the historic city, there is something for everyone in Adelaide.

Oceania.name: Airlie Beach
Oceania.overview:
The white sands of world-famous Whitehaven Beach, glass-bottom-boat day tours on the turquoise waters of the Coral Sea, high-speed catamaran joy rides and Hamilton Island region magic awaits Airlie Beach cruises with Carnival. 
 Travel to the heart of the 74 islands that comprise the picture-perfect Whitsunday Islands, an Australian treasure. 
 Snorkel from the Great Barrier Reef pontoon on an Airlie Beach 4, 7, 9, or 10-day cruise departing Sydney or Brisbane. Your fantastic holiday begins the moment you step onboard Carnival’s cruise ships. There’s a multitude of tastes, sights, natural wonders, sightseeing, themed events and year-round fun to suit the whole family.

Oceania.name: Albany
Oceania.overview:
Get ready to take a step back in time - Albany is the oldest township on the west coast of Australia with a rich and colourful history.

This charming historic town is nestled into the beautiful King George Sound overlooking the Princess Royal Harbour. Once a penal outpost and the first European settlement in Western Australia, over the years Albany has served as a busy trading port, coaling station, and commercial centre for farmlands, and functioned as the epicentre of the west coast whaling industry.

Today, remnants of the town’s history can be seen at every turn, and combine with Albany’s natural beauty to create an irresistible Australian cruise destination. Visitors can explore this one-of-a-kind holiday destination by visiting unmissable Albany highlights including Torndirrup National Park, the Bibbulmun Track, and the town’s many wineries and historic sites.

Oceania.name: Aomori
Oceania.overview: Today, many of the best sights to see in Aomori are the natural kind, as much of the man-made history was lost to various 20 th Century misfortunes. Yet no one seems to have told the cherry blossoms, apple orchards and the mountainous backdrop, all of which greet visitors with a timeless serenity. The region’s two national parks and highly aesthetic Lake Towada are similarly still just doing their thing. In the city, in case you miss Aomori’s annual Nebuta Matsuri Festival, the Nebuta Warasse Museum will let you feast your eyes on some of the incredible illuminated floats that graced the streets. And just outside the city at Seiryu-ji Temple, all 70 feet and 220 tons of Showa Daibutsu statue (“the Great Buddha”) keeps a watchful eye over Aomori.

Oceania.name: Auckland
Oceania.overview:
Auckland, New Zealand’s largest city, is a vibrant metropolis nestled between two stunning harbors, offering a unique blend of urban sophistication and natural beauty. Known as the "City of Sails," it boasts an impressive skyline dotted with volcanic cones and lush green spaces. Visitors can explore a rich cultural scene, including world-class museums, art galleries, and a thriving food and wine culture. Its mild climate and friendly locals make it an ideal destination for travelers seeking adventure, relaxation, and a taste of Kiwi hospitality.

There aren’t too many more beautiful places in the world than the City of Sails, and a Carnival Auckland cruise gives it all to you in style.
 Explore the stunning volcanic cones of the North Island including Mount Eden, and enjoy the magnificent regional parks of the city.
 An Auckland cruise also allows you to experience the cosmopolitan and historic features of New Zealand, including taking in a Maori cultural performance and enjoying some of the best food and wine in the world.
 New Zealand cruise deals with Carnival will give you and your family memories that will last forever.

Oceania.name: Australia
Oceania.overview:
Book one of Carnival Cruise Line’s Australia cruises and enjoy some of most picturesque destinations the country has to offer. 

Take a cruise ship from the Brisbane River or Sydney Harbour Bridge and explore the white sandy beaches of Moreton Island, or dive into a Great Barrier Reef Queensland cruise. 

Soak in the history of Hobart and Port Arthur, the tropical delights of the Kimberley, Broome, and Cairns, or the race that stops a nation in Melbourne with special offers or a majestic cruise deal. 

Whether you’re after a quick getaway, or a longer holiday you’ll remember forever, an Australian cruise holiday with amazing shore excursions is a great way to relax and take some time out.

Oceania.name: Bay of Islands
Oceania.overview:
A Bay of Islands cruise allows you to experience everything that is magical about New Zealand.
 From the famous Hole in the Rock, to the magnificent Haruru Falls, the town of Paihia and the friendly Maori people, a Bay of Islands cruise is something you’ll remember forever.
 Take in the best bits of the 144 islands as part of Carnival’s New Zealand cruises, marvel at the stalactites and stalagmites, or go on a day tour to remember.
 And then there’s the world-class fishing and marine life, the mangrove forest, and the entire natural beauty of the place.

Oceania.name: Brisbane
Oceania.overview:
Brisbane, the vibrant capital of Queensland, is a city that blends modern innovation with natural beauty. Located along the winding Brisbane River, the city boasts a subtropical climate, making it perfect for outdoor exploration. Its skyline features striking modern architecture, complemented by lush green spaces such as the South Bank Parklands and Roma Street Parkland. Known for its friendly locals and laid-back atmosphere, Brisbane offers a dynamic arts scene, world-class dining, and unique attractions like the Story Bridge Adventure Climb, making it a must-visit destination for tourists.

Whether you’re departing or arriving in Brisbane, you’ll be surrounded by an offering of adventure and endless amounts of fun. Think sunshine, relaxation and a holiday the whole family will love!

Oceania.name: Broome
Oceania.overview:
There’s something special about the Kimberley Region, and a Broome cruise can show it all to you. Spend some time in Broome and the Kimberley coast, with a relaxing Broome cruise with Carnival.
 This region of Western Australia is known for its ancient and natural beauty, and a Broome cruise helps you discover it all in style. Cable Beach, the Indian Ocean, Yampi Sound, and the Hunter River will make you feel like Malcolm Douglas, exploring the magnificence of the region.
 The fun and adventure of a Broom cruise includes getting there, with more than 45 onboard activities ensuring the memories will begin long before you arrive.

Oceania.name: Cairns
Oceania.overview:
Cairns, located in Far North Queensland, Australia, is a vibrant city renowned as the gateway to two World Heritage sites: the Great Barrier Reef and the Wet Tropics Rainforest. With a tropical climate, it offers lush landscapes, pristine beaches, and a bustling waterfront esplanade. The city serves as a hub for adventure tourism, including diving, snorkeling, and rainforest excursions. Cairns boasts a diverse cultural scene, with numerous restaurants, bars, and markets reflecting its multicultural community. Its modern infrastructure and proximity to natural wonders make it a must-visit destination for travelers seeking both relaxation and adventure.

Visit the coastal city of Cairns and enjoy a smorgasbord of fun and adventure. A Carnival Cruise Line holiday to Cairns offers the perfect mix of shore excursions the whole family will love - on water and land. Daintree Rainforest tours are the ideal way to experience the ecosystem of the wet tropics of Queensland (a UNESCO World Heritage Site). Go snorkelling on the Great Barrier Reef, experience the Kuranda Scenic Railway and Skyrail, or have a tropical rainforest nature park adventure. A day trip to Cairns itself is always worth it, with amazing shopping on the Esplanade and scenic walks that will blow you away.

Explore the wonders of North Queensland with a cruise to Cairns’ Yorkeys Knob. 

Visit the stunning Yorkeys Knob beach and spend the day relaxing and unwinding.

Oceania.name: Cruise Fiordland National Park
Oceania.overview:
A Fiordland National Park cruise will take you inside some of the most impressive wilderness areas and landscapes in the world. A Fiordland National Park cruise visits the 1.2-million-hectare world heritage site, full of picture-postcard Mountains, lakes, fiords and rainforests.
 A must have photo in the National Park is Mitre Peak, a breathtaking mountain that soars 1692m above sea level!

Oceania.name: Cruise Great Oyster Bay
Oceania.overview: Great Oyster Bay is regarded by many as one of the most breathtaking coastal areas in Australia. Located on the east coast of Tasmania, opening onto the Tasman Sea, the wide, sheltered bay provides stunning views of Freycinet National Park. If you’re lucky, you might even spot dolphins, seals or even whales during migration season.

Oceania.name: Cruise Hubbard Glacier
Oceania.overview: Cruises to Hubbard Glacier let you to take in one of North America’s largest. At a full 76 miles long and six miles wide, Hubbard Glacier — known as a surge glacier or “galloping glacier” — is definitely still galloping. (What we’re saying is that it’s actually growing in size.) You’ll view the glacier from the waters of Disenchantment Bay… but don’t read too much into the name! Snowcapped mountains in the distance offer a spectacular backdrop to viewings of this immense glacier — take it all in from the open decks of the ship, or even enjoy it from your personal balcony. You may even catch wildlife sightings (orcas, humpback whales, bald eagles, anyone?) in the surrounding areas — bonus!

Oceania.name: Cruise Mutiny on the Bounty
Oceania.overview: This destination is a little different than any other! It's actually a series of small island and islets, none of which are inhabited. But they were made famous by an 18th Century mission by the Royal Navy's HMS Bounty, commanded by William Bligh, when the crew seized control of the ship and set them adrift. The story was turned into multiple movies, so we'll swing by and have a look at this historic spot — which is today rather tranquil — as we cruise on by.

Oceania.name: Dravuni Island
Oceania.overview:
Dravuni Island puts you on one of the most magnificent of all the South Pacific Islands, and that’s saying something. Dravuni Island is in the middle of the Great Astrolobe Reef – one of the best coral reefs in the world – south of Fiji’s main island.
 Dravuni Island is home to about 150 people, who love visitors and are always happy to show their tropical paradise off. With a Dravuni Island cruise you can while away the hours with a swim and snorkel, or spend the day enjoying the white sand on the immaculate South Pacific Island beaches. And don’t forget to grab some of the tastiest tropical fruit you’ll ever eat.

Oceania.name: Eden, Australia
Oceania.overview:
A coastal town on the South Coast of New South Wales, Eden is just as beautiful as its name suggests. The area is a natural paradise with stunning coastline, pristine national parks and plenty of wildlife. 

Learn about local history and the legend of Old Tom on Eden’s fascinating Killer Whale Trail. Take a cruise around Twofold Bay for stunning scenery, pristine bays and beaches and the chance to spot dolphins or seals.

Oceania.name: Fremantle (Perth)
Oceania.overview:
Dynamic and vibrant, Fremantle is renowned for its cosmopolitan atmosphere with strong southern European, particularly Italian, influences. A port city on the mouth of the Swan River, Fremantle is popular for its maritime history, laid-back atmosphere and unique culture that permeates the city.

Visitors can take a guided tour to explore the highlights of the area, followed by a cruise down the famous Swan River. Wildlife lovers will enjoy a trip to Rockingham, to see wild dolphins, sea lions and Little Penguins. Famous for its beautiful beaches and cute inhabitants, Quokkas, a visit to Rottnest Island is not to be missed.

Oceania.name: Hakodate
Oceania.overview: It may be hard to imagine today, but Japan once isolated itself from the rest of the world… and this lasted 200 years. This essentially ended at the remote seaport of Hakodate, which led the reopening effort by being the first to welcome visitors from the west. And that’s still happening today! Hakodate offers a window into a unique culture as the traditional home of the Ainu people, whose dress, food and even looks place them a bit outside of mainstream Japanese culture. For example, they favor cooked fish over sushi… and on that note, Hakodate’s four-block long street market is a strollable local source for fresh fish and shellfish. One historical mainstay is Goryokaku Park, a star-shaped island built in 1864 for the defense of the city, best viewed today from its own observation tower. More lofty views are available from Mt. Hakodate, but don’t worry if you’re not up for mountain climbing — cable cars will get you there. But back at ground level don’t miss dining, drinking and shopping at the lively Kanemori Red Brick Warehouse District, whose distinct industrial structures date back to 1909.

Oceania.name: Hamilton Island
Oceania.overview:
Hamilton Island is a tropical paradise nestled in the heart of the Whitsundays, off the coast of Queensland, Australia. It’s the largest inhabited island in the Whitsundays and is a gateway to the world-famous Great Barrier Reef, making it a must-visit destination for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

 The island offers stunning beaches, including the picturesque Catseye Beach, and is surrounded by crystal-clear waters, perfect for snorkeling, diving, and sailing. It’s home to luxurious resorts, a bustling marina, and fantastic dining options, providing the perfect blend of relaxation and excitement.

Oceania.name: Hiroshima
Oceania.overview: Hiroshima’s defining moment came with the dropping of the first atomic bomb. If you think they’d want to put this in the past, the ruined Genbaku Dome disagrees, defiantly standing as a preserved reminder of the event, while the broader Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park symbolizes hope for the future. Nearby Miyajima Island and Shukkeien Gardens provide more serene ways to spend your time, the former making Japan’s top-three scenic locations, and the latter a unique landscape of miniatures modeled after forests, valleys and mountains in China.

Oceania.name: Kangaroo Island
Oceania.overview:
Bursting with natural beauty and teeming with native wildlife, Kangaroo Island is a nature lover’s dream. Located just 15 kilometres from the South Australian mainland, the island is regarded by many as Australia’s “Galápagos”, and with good reason: no other place allows you to walk amongst endangered Australian sea lions, watch kangaroos feed, and catch a glimpse of a shy platypus - all in one day!

Take a tour either by land or sea and venture into the heart of this incredible wildlife sanctuary to the most beautiful sites on the island. Sample local delicacies at wineries, visit Seal Bay Conservation Park, or wander through Flinders Chase National Park to discover the island’s two most popular landmarks, Remarkable Rocks and Admirals Arch.

Oceania.name: Lautoka
Oceania.overview:
Lautoka is Fiji’s second-largest city, located just 24 km north of Nadi on the largest island of Viti Levu. It is also known as ‘Sugar City’ for the surrounding sugarcane fields, the region’s staple industry. Set against the beautiful backdrop of Mt Koroyanitu (Mt Evans), Lautoka is home to an array of authentic and international restaurants and beautiful tropical oases just waiting to be explored. 

Spend the day exploring the deep blue waters on a high-speed catamaran, relax in nature’s original day spa – Fiji’s natural thermal mud pools, or take a highlights tour to soak up all the history, culture and natural beauty. Adventure lovers can set sail for the famous Mamanuca Islands or soar higher, faster and further than ever above rainforest at the Tau Zipline.

Oceania.name: Lifou
Oceania.overview:
A Lifou cruise allows you to experience a land of scenic contrast and natural beauty. As part of New Caldeonia’s Loyalty Island, a cruise to Lifou means enjoying one of the most beautiful places in the South Pacific. Crystal clear waters, white sands, and coral reefs are only part of the story.
 Take a Lifou cruise and enjoy the stunning local fare, amazing guided tours, and views to die for. On a Lifou cruise in the South Pacific Islands, you can swim with the very friendly fish in the marine reserve, visit the secret grotto, and enjoy everything the tropical paradise has to offer.

Oceania.name: Lyttelton (Christchurch)
Oceania.overview:
Arriving at the Christchurch cruise port in nearby Lyttleton, the spectacular start of your Christchurch cruise will be an indication of the amazing day to come.
 Enjoy the natural beauty of the city, its beaches, and its bays, or take an iconic punt along the Avon River.
 Spend your time making the most of everything the city centre has to offer, with historic architecture, amazing arts and crafts, and stylish galleries.
 And don’t forget the food. A Christchurch cruise isn’t complete without a meal at one of the Garden City’s famous restaurants.

Oceania.name: Melbourne
Oceania.overview:
Melbourne, the capital city of Victoria, Australia, is renowned for its vibrant arts scene, diverse culinary offerings, and rich cultural heritage. Situated at the northern end of Port Phillip Bay, the city spans approximately 9,900 square kilometers, extending over 40 kilometers to the south, 30 kilometers to the east, and 20 kilometers to the north. As of 2021, Melbourne's population exceeded 5 million, with over 40% of residents born overseas, reflecting its multicultural fabric. The city's dynamic atmosphere, characterized by a blend of historic architecture and modern developments, offers visitors a unique urban experience.

Get ready to set sail on an unforgettable cruise adventure to or from Melbourne, Australia’s vibrant capital of Victoria. Whether you’re arriving to explore or departing on your next journey, Melbourne promises an exciting start or end to your cruise. Brimming with art, sport, and mouth-watering cuisine, this city is a must-visit on your cruise itinerary. Melbourne isn’t just Australia’s second most populated city; it's a playground of fun! From the iconic Melbourne Cricket Ground to the World Heritage-listed Royal Exhibition Building and the National Gallery of Victoria, there’s no shortage of attractions. Take a leisurely stroll along the Yarra River, explore the bustling Southbank, or hop on public transport to visit Port Melbourne. With our majestic cruises, you're guaranteed an immersive experience in the heart of Melbourne’s charm and vivacity, whether you're cruising to or from this dynamic city.

Oceania.name: Moorea
Oceania.overview:
There aren’t many more beautiful places in the word than Moorea, and a Moorea cruise allows you to discover why. A Moorea cruise to the island that sits just north of Tahiti in French Polynesia is all about relaxation. The beautiful island is surrounded by a barrier reef, which means crystal clear waters, marine life like you’ve never seen before, and some of the best snorkeling in the world.
 A Moorea cruise is also about experiencing the island itself. Moorea has some impressive natural landscapes and some of the best scenery in the region. Tall mountains and fields of tropical flowers make hiking a spectacular adventure at every turn. Visit Moorea and put a Transpacific cruise on your list today.

Oceania.name: Mystery Island
Oceania.overview:
Have you ever dreamed of escaping to a secluded, uninhabited island, far removed from the rest of the world? If so, a Mystery Island cruise with onboard activities from Sydney or Brisbane, Australia is the perfect adventure for you. This remote part of Vanuatu is exclusively visited by cruise passengers on cruise ships, offering a unique getaway experience.
 Despite its lack of permanent residents, Mystery Island is brimming with shore excursions. The island gained popularity following a visit from Queen Elizabeth II in the 1970s, and it has since become a sought-after retreat. With its stunning beaches, crystal-clear waters, and some of the region's best snorkelling spots, a Vanuatu cruise to the South Pacific islands is an absolute must.

Oceania.name: Naha, Okinawa
Oceania.overview: Naha is a city with a long history of welcoming visitors ashore — this onetime capital of the Ryukyu Kingdom has been a seaport since the 1400s. For a city with such a history things may seem surprisingly modern, because most of the classic buildings were destroyed in battles near the end of World War II, and historic ruins do remain. But there’s a ton of nature to see… and it’s beautiful like always, whether you’re exploring the million-ish stalagmites and stalactites of Gyokusendo Cave, the scenic views of Cape Manzamo’s grassy plain, or Ryukyu Village’s comfy harmony with its Okinawan surroundings. But don’t forget that the newly-built parts of Naha are worth exploring too, and Kokusai Street measures over one strollable mile of things to do, see and eat before you move on to explore more of the Okinawan region.

Oceania.name: Napier
Oceania.overview:
Jump on a Carnival Napier cruise and visit one of the most impressive cities on New Zealand’s North Island. Napier is the Art Deco capital of the world, with stunning Art Deco buildings and even an Art Deco festival. But a Napier cruise us about more than just the amazing architecture.
 The Hawkes Bay region is also known for its stunning beaches and beautiful wine tours. Napier cruise tours will also allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of the region, including the Tutaekuri River. Take a Napier cruise with Carnival and create memories that will last forever for you and your family.

Oceania.name: New Zealand
Oceania.overview:
Explore the natural and cultural wonders of the North and South Island of New Zealand.
 New Zealand cruises include cruises to Fiordland Park, Mildford Sound, the Bay of Islands and close-up views of their natural beauty.
 Take a 10, 11, or 12 night New Zealand cruise and enjoy the adventure the land of the long white cloud is known for.
 Immerse yourself in Māori culture, experience the Art Deco decadence in Napier, and cruise around some of the most picturesque places in the world.
 There’s a lot of things to do in New Zealand from a cruise ship.

Oceania.name: Norfolk Island
Oceania.overview: Norfolk Island can trace its history back to at least the 18th Century and the legendary Captain Cook. Its true lineage, though, starts a few decades — and attempted settlements — later, when the village that became Norfolk was founded by natives of nearby Pitcairn Island and descendants of sailors from another piece of maritime history: the "Mutiny on the Bounty" incident. Given all this, history unsurprisingly abounds at the Norfolk Island Museum, while great examples of the island's native forest and iconic pine trees can be found within the Norfolk Island National Park. Another great pair of sights — the tree-lined cliffs and picturesque lagoons — can be found right on the coast, making a convincing case for Norfolk as a destination of unspoiled natural beauty.

Oceania.name: Noumea
Oceania.overview:
Embark on an unforgettable South Pacific adventure to the heart of Noumea on one of our cruise ships. The 'Paris of the Pacific', Noumea, promises more sunshine days than any other Pacific Island capital. 
 With its white sandy beaches, crystal clear waters, and vibrant culture, a cruise to Noumea, the capital of New Caledonia, from either Sydney or Brisbane is guaranteed to give you an experience that transcends the ordinary. Enjoy exploring the hustle and bustle of this thriving South Pacific Island and the fusion of the French and Melanesian flavours during one of our shore excursions. Immerse in the Kanak culture, from traditional music and dance to arts and crafts. The Kanak culture is a vibrant tapestry that reflects the rich heritage of New Caledonia.

Oceania.name: Nuku'alofa
Oceania.overview:
Tonga is known as the Friendly Islands, and its capital – Nuku’Alofa – shows you why. A Nuku’Alofa cruise will put you in the heart of the tropical paradise, and allow you to take in everything that the Transpacific offers.

Enjoy the local hospitality with a Nuku’Alofa cruise, including the delicious food and drink. Swim in the blue waters, dive the shipwrecks, and tour the local reefs. A cruise to Nuku’Alofa also means touring the island and taking in the stunning local history and culture. Take a boat and explore the nearby islands, or visit the amazing Royal Palace. 

A Nuku’Alofa, Tonga cruise is also about getting there, with more than 45 activities onboard meaning the fun and adventure never stops.

Oceania.name: Otaru (Sapporo)
Oceania.overview: Herring fishing! Coal mining! And would you believe… even music box manufacturing? Otaru, located on the island of Hokkaido, has certainly had a history of boom cycles, but the herring era, when its waters supplied all of Asia, holds an important place in the city’s history. After all, icons remain in the form of 19th century tycoons’ “herring mansions” — like the coast-hugging Otaru-shi Nishin Goden — which still draw the fascination of visitors. But even outside of history itself, the island’s scenic natural attractions, like national parks and its rugged west coast, have a timeless appeal. The freshness of the sushi is of course unsurprising, while the local whiskey distillery — perhaps surprisingly — uses traditional Scottish methods to produce a smoky malt. Don’t miss exploring the capital city, nearby Sapporo, and its spots for taking in the best sights, like Hitsujigaoka Observation Hill at Hokkaido Agricultural Experimental Station, which shows off the pastureland of Sapporo’s outskirts, and also the 482-foot Sapporo TV Tower at Sapporo Odori Park, which shares with the curious visitor some amazing city views, day and night.

Oceania.name: Papeete, Tahiti
Oceania.overview: Papeete is the capital of French Polynesia, and sits on the island of Tahiti. A Papeete cruise means making the most of everything the stunning South Pacific has to offer. A Tahiti cruise from Australia will put you among some amazingly rich culture, and some very friendly locals. Enjoy the stunning churches and the thriving centre of the city. The Robert Wan Pearl Museum is a must-see for a Papeete cruise, as is the famous municipal market. Swim in the crystal-clear waters, stroll the sandy beaches, and hike the rugged mountains.

Oceania.name: Phillip Island
Oceania.overview:
Located south-east of Melbourne Phillip Island is bursting at the seams with postcard-worthy sights and experiences. From captivating coastline, beautiful beaches, an abundance of wildlife, motorsport highlights, history, and adrenalin activities, you’ll fall in love with this charming Australian cruise destination. 

Nature lovers have long been flocking to Phillip Island and with Australia’s largest fur sea colony, impressive rock formations and breathtaking views, who can blame them? Whether you’re gliding within centimetres of thousands of seals playing in the water and sunbaking on nearby rocks, or admiring the view from Cape Woolamai, Phillip Island's highest point, you’re sure to make memories that will last a lifetime. 

Once you’ve said hello to the local wildlife explore Phillip Island from a different perspective atop the SkyTrail’s 10.3-metre-high ropes course or with a visit to the Phillip Island Grand Prix circuit, National Vietnam Veterans Museum or Rusty Water Brewery. There truly is something for everyone on Phillip Island.

Oceania.name: Picton
Oceania.overview:
A Picton cruise with Carnival takes you into one of the most picturesque places in New Zealand, and that’s saying something. The town of Picton is the entrance of ferries, cruise ships, and pleasure cruises to the South Island. Shakespeare Bay and Waitohi Wharf are the town’s main ports, but Picton cruise tours are about much more.
 The town of Picton is the start of the world-famous Marlborough wine region, and has a multitude of taste-tempting restaurants, cafes and epicurean delights. A Picton cruise in 2021 and 2022 with Carnival also allows you to experience the region’s stunning Maori culture, and some impressive New Zealand countryside.

Oceania.name: Port Chalmers (Dunedin)
Oceania.overview:
A Dunedin cruise puts you in the heart of one of the South lsland’s largest cities, and is the perfect way to enjoy the culture, history, and architecture of the city. Once you sail into the Dunedin cruise port at Port Chalmers, you’ll know exactly what we mean.
 Carnival offers a lot of shore excursions around the world, and Dunedin tours are some of the best. Visit the impressive Larnach Castle or witness the graceful fur seals off the Otago Peninsula.
 The city centre itself is well worth the trip. Stroll around the botanical gardens and make the most of Dunedin’s thriving café and restaurant scene. Or board the Taieri Gorge Train and take in the natural wonders that a Dunedin cruise provides.

Oceania.name: Port Denarau
Oceania.overview:
Port Denarau encapsulates everything that is perfect about Fiji. The Port Denarau cruise terminal is the entrance to total relaxation, whether it’s on the golf course, the white sandy beaches, the amazing culture, or the stunning island resorts. And don’t forget to take advantage of the amazing shopping.
 A Port Denarau cruise day trip lets you enjoy the beauty of the coast of Fiji, and soak in the modern features that make it such a sought-after destination.

Oceania.name: Port Douglas
Oceania.overview:
Port Douglas, a coastal town in Queensland, Australia, is renowned for its pristine beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and lush rainforests. Located approximately 60 kilometers north of Cairns, it serves as a gateway to the Great Barrier Reef and the Daintree Rainforest. The town offers a blend of natural beauty and modern amenities, making it a popular destination for tourists seeking both relaxation and adventure. Visitors can enjoy activities such as snorkeling, diving, and exploring the local wildlife, all within a tropical climate that boasts warm temperatures year-round.

Is the Isle of Man in the United Kingdom? No… but also kind of yes. Look, it’s complicated but the capital, Douglas, is a destination that started off in the 1800s as a vacation resort (they call it “holiday,” of course), attracting visitors from all around the area with seaside delights like dramatic coastlines, great beaches and nature aplenty. Today there’s so much to see and it’s all so beautiful, with castles, parks, nature reserves and more. But the past is never far, and a rather plentiful collection of museums puts it all within reach, whether your interests tend towards the nautical, aviation, military… or human, including the Isle of Man’s Celtic and Viking days.

Few places on Earth can rival the breathtaking beauty of Port Douglas, and the most remarkable way to experience it is through a Carnival Port Douglas cruise. Depart from Sydney or Brisbane and embrace the Great Barrier Reef, embark on a sunset cruise, delve into the world heritage-listed Daintree forest and national park, or dive into the outer Agincourt reef for an underwater adventure.
 The humid tropics of North Queensland are home to an incredible array of flora and fauna. Opt for a full-day tour, a reef cruise, or a visit to the Mossman Gorge. Alternatively, you might prefer to unwind in the ambience of Port Douglas itself. Explore shops and cafes, go snorkelling, bask on the Australian beaches, and see why tourists from all over are drawn to this charming locale.

Oceania.name: Port Lincoln
Oceania.overview:
Nestled into the southern tip of South Australia’s Eyre Peninsula, Port Lincoln is a natural and historical wonderland. Abounding with museums, art galleries, and fantastic fishing spots, the bustling town is known by many as “Australia’s seafood capital” - and for good reason. The waters around the unspoilt coastline are teeming with whiting, snapper, salmon, garfish and squid. 

And Port Lincoln isn’t just a haven for foodies. Nature lovers and history buffs alike will also love Port Lincoln’s fascinating maritime history, breathtaking geological formations and scenic sites. 

No Port Lincoln visit would be complete without checking out the rugged coastline, stunning lookouts, and unspoilt beaches. At Winters Hill lookout, you’ll enjoy gorgeous views over Port Lincoln and the local marina precinct, while at Fishery Bay lookout you’ll be treated to panoramic views of wide beaches and rolling seas. Keen to meet the local wildlife? Head to Cape Wiles, where you may be lucky enough to spot a colony of New Zealand fur seals playing near the rocks.

Oceania.name: Port Vila
Oceania.overview:
If there was ever a South Pacific city built for visiting, it’s Port Vila. A Carnival Port Vila cruise sails into the Port Vila cruise terminal on the south western coast and into the heart of the capital of Vanuatu. A shore excursion as part of a Port Vila cruise opens up everything this beautiful part of the world has to offer.
 Enjoy the natural beauty, the stunning beaches, the clear waters, and the amazing welcoming locals of the South Pacific Islands. A cruise to Port Vila will make you feel at home straight away, and there is so much to do for the whole family, year-round.

Oceania.name: Queenstown
Oceania.overview: Nestled on the shores of Lake Wakatipu, Queenstown is a picturesque resort town on New Zealand's South Island. Surrounded by the dramatic Southern Alps, it is renowned for its stunning natural landscapes, adrenaline-pumping adventure activities, and vibrant dining and nightlife. Known as the “Adventure Capital of the World,” Queenstown offers bungee jumping, skydiving, jet boating, and hiking opportunities for thrill-seekers. Its bustling town center features boutique shops, world-class restaurants, and charming cafes, making it a year-round destination for relaxation and exploration alike.

Oceania.name: Shimizu (Mt. Fuji)
Oceania.overview: The snowcapped Mount Fuji may just be the single most iconic image of Japan, a breathtaking symbol that towers over the landscape at over 12,000 feet. The active volcano inspires just about everyone who’s seen it, and many continue to hold it sacred. Its foothills are dotted with Shinto shrines welcoming visitors and those on their way up to Fuji. Miho-no-Matsubara is considered to have among the best views of the mountain, which is just one of the things that the seaport of Shimizu, located in Shizuoka City, can boast. On the other hand, the ruins of Sumpu Castle are a 400-year-old symbol of Japan’s Edo period, a time of culture that introduced some of its most iconic art styles… examples of which are on display at Tokaido Hiroshige Art Museum. Shimizu is also Japan’s tuna fishing capital, with its freshness making it a popular choice in local restaurants.

Oceania.name: South Pacific
Oceania.overview:
There aren’t too many places in the world more beautiful than those you’ll visit on a Carnival South Pacific island cruise.

Soak up the white sands, the pristine beaches, and the tropical paradises that only South Pacific cruises can deliver.
Make memories that will last forever as you scuba dive in pristine waters, take in the local culture, and relax at any of our South Pacific cruise destinations.
And do it all on a ship that has more than 45 activities onboard for you and your family to enjoy.

Oceania.name: Suva
Oceania.overview:
A Suva cruise from Australia is the perfect way to relax, unwind, and take in everything the South Pacific has to offer. And it has a lot to offer. A cruise to Suva, the capital city of Fiji, allows you to explore the natural and cultural beauties of the island of Viti Levu.
 A Suva cruise offers something for everyone, with a modern CBD mixed in with traditional sari shops and traders, and colonial buildings throughout. A Suva cruise allows you to experience the amazing local culture, the yummy local cuisine, and the stunning scenery. A cruise to Suva, Fiji is one you and your family will never forget.

Oceania.name: Sydney
Oceania.overview:
Sydney, Australia’s largest city, is a dazzling coastal metropolis renowned for its iconic landmarks, vibrant neighborhoods, and stunning natural beauty. Nestled around a sparkling harbor, it offers a unique blend of urban sophistication and outdoor adventures. Visitors are drawn to world-famous attractions like the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge, while golden beaches such as Bondi and Manly provide a quintessential Aussie experience. With a multicultural population and a thriving culinary scene, Sydney is a dynamic and welcoming destination that promises something for every traveler.

Australia’s famous city isn’t the only Sydney worth cruising to! While exploring Canada & New England on a Carnival cruise, you can dig deeper into a pocket of Canadian history, a city that was actually founded in the late 18th century by loyalist British soldiers retreating from the American Revolution. Robust industries fueled by the region’s natural resources sprang up, leading to the place you’ll enjoy exploring today. Start at the harbor — you can’t miss it because of the huge fiddle! It’s the world’s largest and hints at the live music (sea shanties, anyone?) you might experience while dining at the waterfront restaurants enjoying freshly-caught seafood. There’s actual history to explore here, with the entire Fortress of Louisbourg having been painstakingly recreated from its original rubble to showcase a pivotal location from the Seven Years’ War. Alexander Graham Bell’s house is another shouldn’t-miss for fans of the inventive spirit that spurred modern communications as we know it.

Oceania.name: Tangalooma - Moreton Island
Oceania.overview: A Carnival Tangalooma, Moreton Island cruise from Sydney to Tangalooma Island Resort gives you access to one of Australia’s best-kept secrets. Experience snorkeling the famous shipwrecks or go whale watching for an experience you’ll never forget. A 4-day Tangalooma, Moreton Island Cruise from Sydney or Brisbane also offers the chance to take an exhilarating 4WD bus adventure around Tangalooma, Moreton Island, the 3rd largest sand island in the world! From sand tobogganing down the dunes, ATV quad bike tours and scenic helicopter flights to spotting turtles on a clear bottom kayak tour or even a spot of parasailing, this cruise has something for everyone. And enjoy more than 45 onboard activities on our Tangalooma, Moreton Island Cruise.

Oceania.name: Tauranga
Oceania.overview:
A Carnival Tauranga cruise allows you to enjoy everything the magnificent Bay of Plenty has to offer. In the North Island, Tauranga is one of New Zealand’s fastest-growing cities, and it’s easy to see why. Use our shore excursions to take in the Maori culture of the region, or venture to the Polynesian spa and hot springs.
 Hike the impressive Mount Maunganui and enjoy the amazing views, or visit the Rotorua region and lake. Downtown Tauranga is also a big attraction. Cruise to New Zealand to embark on a Tauranga cruise and enjoy the cultural and foodie delights of a city known for its art and restaurants.

Oceania.name: Transpacific
Oceania.overview: Some people see an ocean and they’re like… “You know what, I’ve gotta cross it. Let’s see what’s on the other side.” If you’re an explorer, it’s just what you do. Today we know exactly what’s where (thanks, maps!) but making the journey still matters, and the spirit of discovery lives on in a Transpacific Carnival cruise. If white sand and turquoise waters are your style, choose an itinerary featuring islands whose names equal “exotic” — Fiji, Tahiti, Hawaii. Or go for a vacation that gets you deep into destinations as diverse as Alaska and Japan… with a little Indonesia and Philippines on the side. Either way, this Pacific Ocean crossing will give you plenty of epic names to cross off your travel to-do list.

Oceania.name: Wellington
Oceania.overview:
Wellington, the capital city of New Zealand, is a vibrant and picturesque destination nestled between rolling hills and a sparkling harbor. Known as the "Coolest Little Capital," it boasts a lively arts scene, world-class dining, and breathtaking natural landscapes. The city is compact and walkable, offering stunning views at every turn, from the iconic Mount Victoria Lookout to the bustling Wellington Waterfront. With its mix of modern attractions and traditional Maori culture, Wellington captivates visitors with its charm and unique atmosphere, making it a must-visit destination in the Pacific.

No cruise to New Zealand is complete without a visit to windy Wellington. But don’t let that title fool you. A Wellington cruise opens up some of the most spectacular Carnival experiences available. A Wellington cruise means enjoying the nature, culture, shopping, and excitement the city is famous for.
 Enjoy a craft beer and an amazing meal on the harbour, tour the Te Papa National Museum, visit the Carter Observatory, or take in The Lord of the Rings celebrations at the Weta Workshop Wellington location. A visit to the Wellington Botanic Gardens is also a must-do, as is an always-memorable trip to the amazing Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary in the heart of Wellington.

Oceania.name: Willis Island (Onboard Experience)
Oceania.overview: Established in 1921, the weather monitoring station is home to four weather observers and provides a cyclone early warning service for Queensland. This tiny island, located 450 kilometres east of Cairns, is home to fascinating wildlife, as well as a manned Australian Bureau of Meteorology weather monitoring station. While the cruise does not include a stop at Willis Island, you can catch glimpses of its natural beauty from the comfort of the ship. As you sail by, you'll have the opportunity to witness some amazing wildlife that calls this island home, including wedge-tailed shearwaters and sooty terns.

South & East Asia.name: Airlie Beach
South & East Asia.overview:
The white sands of world-famous Whitehaven Beach, glass-bottom-boat day tours on the turquoise waters of the Coral Sea, high-speed catamaran joy rides and Hamilton Island region magic awaits Airlie Beach cruises with Carnival. 
 Travel to the heart of the 74 islands that comprise the picture-perfect Whitsunday Islands, an Australian treasure. 
 Snorkel from the Great Barrier Reef pontoon on an Airlie Beach 4, 7, 9, or 10-day cruise departing Sydney or Brisbane. Your fantastic holiday begins the moment you step onboard Carnival’s cruise ships. There’s a multitude of tastes, sights, natural wonders, sightseeing, themed events and year-round fun to suit the whole family.

South & East Asia.name: Asia
South & East Asia.overview: Explore the multiculturally diverse South-East-Asian region. Enjoy a range of cruises that offer unlimited fun, and unimaginable sights, sounds, tastes and family-style adventure with Carnival cruises to or from Asia. Sail away to some of the most exciting and exotic cities in the world and immerse your senses with over 45 daily activities and a huge variety of dining options. Discover amazing dining, shopping, ancient temples and shrines, onboard cruises to Asia.

South & East Asia.name: Bangkok
South & East Asia.overview:
Bangkok, Thailand’s bustling capital, is a dynamic metropolis that seamlessly blends tradition and modernity. Renowned for its ornate temples, vibrant street markets, and dazzling skyscrapers, the city offers a unique cultural experience. Visitors are drawn to its lively neighborhoods like Sukhumvit and Silom, each offering a mix of entertainment, dining, and shopping. The Chao Phraya River flows through the city, providing picturesque views and opportunities for boat tours. Bangkok’s energetic pace and rich heritage make it an unforgettable destination for all travelers.

Laem Chabang is your gateway to Bangkok.

An enchanting city on the Chao Phraya River, when you make it to Bangkok, you know you have truly made it to the essence of Thailand. Graceful dancers perform in shimmering silk gowns, temples with gold-leaf spires and priceless Buddhas wait to be discovered, and riverboats cruise a maze of busy canals. Visitors can shop the local markets, take a tuk-tuk ride and tour the Grand Palace, visit a Buddhist monastery or float down the Chao Phraya River and Thonburi Canals.

South & East Asia.name: Beijing
South & East Asia.overview: Beijing, China's capital city, is a dynamic metropolis that seamlessly blends its imperial past with modern innovation. As one of the world’s most populous cities, it is renowned for its sprawling palaces, ancient temples, and contemporary skyscrapers. Visitors can explore its bustling hutongs, sample authentic Chinese cuisine, and experience its vibrant arts and cultural scene. The city is also home to iconic landmarks like the Forbidden City and Tiananmen Square. With efficient public transport and a diverse array of attractions, Beijing offers a captivating experience for every traveler.

South & East Asia.name: Busan (Pusan), South Korea
South & East Asia.overview:
Welcome to Busan, South Korea’s second-largest city which is home to mountains, beaches, hot springs and incredible culture.

With so much on offer it can be hard deciding what to do in Busan. You might want to visit one of the most striking landmarks, the Haedong Yonggungsa Temple, perched by the sea with a dramatic rocky backdrop. For a look at everyday life, you could explore the colourful, winding alleyways of the Gamcheon Cultural Village which was built on such a steep mountain-side slope it’s been nicknamed the “Machu Picchu of Busan.” Foodies will be spoilt for choice too at the Jagalchi Fish Market.

South & East Asia.name: Darwin
South & East Asia.overview:
Cruise across the Top End in style with a Carnival Darwin cruise. Enjoy a cruise to Darwin, and make the most of everything the laid-back capital of the Northern Territory has to offer.
 Take in the city’s beaches, buzzing markets and stunning air conditioned restaurants and cafes. With a Darwin cruise you can also enjoy the marinas, jetties and waterfront parks that have made the city so popular with visitors.
 And don’t miss the city’s amazing natural beauty, including the Charles Darwin National Park, almost alongside the Darwin cruise terminal. The fun and adventure will start before you get there, with more than 45 activities onboard a Darwin cruise, helping you make memories that will last a lifetime.

South & East Asia.name: Denpasar
South & East Asia.overview:
Bali, often referred to as the "Island of the Gods," is a tropical paradise nestled in Indonesia's archipelago. Known for its stunning beaches, lush green landscapes, and vibrant culture, Bali offers a unique blend of natural beauty and modern amenities. Its dynamic cities, like Denpasar and Ubud, cater to tourists with an array of restaurants, boutiques, and entertainment. Bali is also famous for its spirituality, evident in its iconic temples and traditional ceremonies. Whether you're seeking adventure or tranquility, Bali has something for everyone.

There’s a reason so many people love Bali cruises, and it’s easy to see why.
 
 Enjoy the white sand beaches, visit sacred temples, experience active volcanoes and ancient rice paddies and soak in everything Benoa (Bali) has to offer.
 And make the most of our Bali cruises with more than 45 onboard activities, with a lot of fun and adventure for people of all ages.

South & East Asia.name: Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
South & East Asia.overview: Kuala Lumpur, the vibrant capital of Malaysia, is a bustling metropolis that seamlessly blends modernity with rich cultural heritage. Spanning 243 square kilometers, it is the nation's largest city, serving as the central hub for cultural, financial, and economic activities. The city's skyline is dominated by iconic structures like the Petronas Twin Towers, symbolizing its rapid development. Kuala Lumpur's diverse population contributes to a multicultural atmosphere, reflected in its festivals, cuisine, and architecture. As a leading destination in Southeast Asia, it offers visitors a dynamic urban experience amidst a tapestry of traditions.

South & East Asia.name: Fukuoka (Hakata)
South & East Asia.overview: Hakata is overflowing with fascinating history, serene temples and spectacular scenery. Outside Hakata, Kokura Castle is surrounded by a beautiful garden where visitors can witness a traditional tea ceremony. Soak up the spectacular scenery on a gondola-like boat called a donko as you cruise along the Yanagawa River. Explore the Moji Historical District, a charming area that sits along Kyushu's waterfront boasting many interesting historic buildings.

South & East Asia.name: Hanoi
South & East Asia.overview: Hanoi, the capital of Vietnam, is a vibrant city where traditional charm meets modern development. Nestled along the banks of the Red River, this bustling metropolis is known for its centuries-old architecture, bustling markets, and serene lakes. The city's districts, such as the historic Old Quarter, are filled with narrow streets that buzz with activity, offering visitors a taste of authentic Vietnamese culture. Hanoi’s culinary scene, iconic landmarks, and welcoming locals make it a must-visit destination for travelers seeking a mix of history, culture, and modern flair.

South & East Asia.name: Ho Chi Minh City
South & East Asia.overview: Ho Chi Minh City, formerly known as Saigon, is Vietnam's largest metropolis, situated along the Saigon River near the Mekong Delta. As the nation’s economic powerhouse, it boasts a dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers, bustling markets, and French colonial architecture. The city is divided into 24 districts, each offering unique attractions and experiences. District 1 serves as the central business and entertainment hub, featuring landmarks like the Ben Thanh Market and the Saigon Opera House. Visitors can enjoy the vibrant street food scene, cultural heritage, and energetic nightlife.

South & East Asia.name: Hong Kong
South & East Asia.overview:
Hong Kong, a vibrant metropolis on China's southern coast, seamlessly blends Eastern traditions with Western modernity. Spanning 1,104 square kilometers, it comprises Hong Kong Island, Kowloon Peninsula, and the New Territories, along with over 200 outlying islands. As a leading global financial hub, Hong Kong boasts a dynamic skyline, bustling harbor, and efficient public transport system. The city's rich cultural tapestry is evident in its diverse culinary scene, traditional festivals, and thriving arts landscape. Visitors can explore bustling markets, serene temples, and verdant hiking trails, all within a compact urban setting.

Imagine a shimmering forest of steel and glass skyscrapers, bustling harbor waters filled with junks and sampans, and the inviting green hills of Kowloon. Welcome to Hong Kong, a top travel destination that never fails to enchant with all its excitement and exotic charm! 

On Hong Kong Island, modern skyscrapers and luxury hotels rise majestically. The narrow streets are alive with noodle vendors and fortunetellers. The countless shops offer everything from bespoke suits and ancient porcelain to the latest in consumer electronics. Over seven million people move at a dizzying pace in this vibrant hub of capitalism, commerce, and enterprise.

With so much to see and do, the hardest part will be choosing what to do first. For a breathtaking view of the entire city, head to Victoria Peak. Savor lunch at one of the city's famous dim sum restaurants. Stroll down the crowded streets to feel the city's energetic pulse. And whether you're in the mood or not—shop. After all, you're in the duty-free capital of the world!

South & East Asia.name: Kathmandu
South & East Asia.overview: Nestled in a valley surrounded by the majestic Himalayas, Kathmandu is Nepal's bustling capital and cultural heart. The city seamlessly blends ancient traditions with modern influences, offering a vibrant mix of historic sites, lively streets, and serene monasteries. Known for its colorful markets, delicious cuisine, and welcoming locals, Kathmandu is a gateway to exploring Nepal's breathtaking landscapes. As a hub of art, music, and spirituality, it attracts travelers seeking adventure and cultural immersion. Its lively atmosphere promises an unforgettable experience for every visitor.

South & East Asia.name: Keelung (Taipei)
South & East Asia.overview:
On a Carnival cruise to Taiwan, guests can explore Keelung, gateway to Taipei. A sophisticated modern metropolis that has not forgotten its rich traditional past, Taipei is the largest city and capital of Taiwan, located on the Danshui River.

For those who love the hustle and bustle of a big city, Taipei truly is a great place to visit. It is renowned for its shopping malls, among the most popular being Taipei 101 Mall. If shopping isn’t your thing explore the quaint, old cobblestone streets of Chiufen village or take in some history at the National Palace Museum. For something peaceful, visit the Tienlai Hot Springs Resort - nestled among the verdant slopes of Yangmingshan National Park or head to Yehliu Geographic Park, known for its otherworldly rock formations.

South & East Asia.name: Koh Samui
South & East Asia.overview:
Of all Thailand’s glorious islands, Koh Samui is one of the most popular, and as soon as you arrive it will be easy to see why. Rich with white sandy beaches, turquoise water, colourful coral reefs and swaying coconut trees, Koh Samui is truly a holiday destination that has it all.

Admire the mighty Wat Phra Yai temple with its 15-metre golden Buddha statue. Or, visit Chaweng Beach, famous for its powdery white sand, crystalline water, and bustling restaurants and bars lining the shore. Then there are also the local markets, waterfalls, temples and shopping galore!

South & East Asia.name: Komodo Island
South & East Asia.overview:
It’s a part of the world that has been described by many as having some of the best beaches in the world. Any cruise to Indonesia should include it. It is the definition of paradise.
 Carnival’s Komodo Island cruise means you can make the most of what is tropical perfection.
 As home of the Komodo dragons, a Komodo Island cruise puts amazing wildlife in front of you, on land and in the ocean.
 A Komodo Island cruise puts you in some of the world’s best diving sites, with remarkable red coral, a stunning manta ray population, and crystal-clear waters.
 With most of the island a UNESCO World Heritage Site, a Komodo island cruise is a must-do for anyone looking to cruise to Asia in 2021 and 2022.

South & East Asia.name: Krong Siem Reap
South & East Asia.overview: Krong Siem Reap, often called the gateway to Angkor Wat, is a vibrant city in northwestern Cambodia known for its rich cultural heritage and lively energy. This charming destination blends modern amenities with traditional Khmer culture, offering visitors an array of attractions, from bustling night markets to tranquil riverside spots. Siem Reap’s appeal lies in its dynamic mix of ancient wonders and contemporary lifestyle, making it a hub for history buffs and adventure seekers alike. Its warm hospitality ensures an unforgettable experience.

South & East Asia.name: Kyoto
South & East Asia.overview:
Kyoto, nestled in a valley surrounded by mountains on three sides, is a city where tradition and modernity coexist harmoniously. As Japan's cultural capital, it boasts over 2,000 temples and shrines, including 17 UNESCO World Heritage Sites. The city's well-preserved machiya townhouses and historic districts like Gion offer glimpses into its rich past. Kyoto's vibrant culinary scene features kaiseki dining and bustling markets such as Nishiki Market. With a population of approximately 1.5 million, Kyoto seamlessly blends historical charm with contemporary urban life.

Even if you’ve never been, there’s a good chance you’ve heard of Kobe — this is the place where the internationally-prized beef is made under famously strict standards. But before Kobe became associated with fine dining, it was one of the first Japanese seaports to open for trading with the West, following the nation’s 200 years of isolation. Technically, the city of Kobe didn’t even exist at that point — not until 1889 — though the area had been settled for over 1,500 years. Kobe today boasts great city views from Mt. Rokko, well-preserved castles and mansions, plus museums of art, literature… and sake, where tastings are available! Shrines, temples and gardens are among the iconic local sights, and some great ones can even be found in Kobe’s regional neighbors Osaka and Kyoto.

South & East Asia.name: Langkawi
South & East Asia.overview:
Translated from Malay, Langkawi means ‘land of the reddish-brown eagle’. We’re not sure what the Malay term for ‘stunning mountainous island with impossibly white beaches and turquoise water’ is, but it could surely be named that, too. 

Langkawi comprises a group of 99 tropical islands lying off the northwestern coast of Peninsular Malaysia. The main island is known as Pulau Langkawi. Langkawi has been accorded Geopark status by UNESCO for its beautiful geological heritage of stunning landscapes, karsts, caves, sea arches, stacks, glacial dropstones, and fossils. The best way to appreciate the impossibly stunning beauty of this place? Take a cable car trip up the mountain, and be ready for your jaw to drop when you reach the top!

South & East Asia.name: Lembar (Lombok)
South & East Asia.overview:
A Carnival cruise to Lembar (Lombok) is the perfect chance to get away from it all and create memories that will last forever. A Lombok cruise reveals an island that features stunning mountains, green pastures and tall trees as far as the eye can see.
 Spend time walking along pristine Senggigi Beach and make the most of the spectacular location. Lose yourself in the Lingsar temple and appreciate the rich cultural traditions of the area.
 A Lombok cruise is also about more than the destination. With more than 45 activities onboard, the fun and adventure will never stop.

South & East Asia.name: Maldive Islands
South & East Asia.overview: Malé, the vibrant capital of the Maldives, is a bustling city that seamlessly blends tradition with modernity. Covering approximately 8.30 square kilometers, it is home to over 211,000 residents, making it one of the world's most densely populated cities. Situated at the southern edge of North Malé Atoll, the city serves as the political, economic, and cultural hub of the nation. Visitors can explore colorful markets, historic mosques, and enjoy the lively waterfront. Despite its small size, Malé offers a rich tapestry of experiences, from local cuisine to vibrant nightlife, providing tourists with an authentic glimpse into Maldivian urban life.

South & East Asia.name: Metro Manila
South & East Asia.overview: Manila, the vibrant capital of the Philippines, is a bustling metropolis that perfectly blends historical charm with modern dynamism. Located on the eastern shore of Manila Bay, this city is a hub of culture, commerce, and entertainment. Its lively streets are filled with bustling markets, high-rise buildings, and iconic landmarks like Rizal Park and Intramuros. Known as the "Pearl of the Orient," Manila showcases a mix of Spanish colonial architecture, vibrant nightlife, and a thriving arts scene, making it a dynamic destination for every traveler.

South & East Asia.name: Mumbai
South & East Asia.overview: Mumbai, often called the "City of Dreams," is a bustling metropolis that perfectly blends tradition and modernity. Located on the western coast of India, this vibrant city is the financial capital of the country and a hub for entertainment, business, and culture. Its skyline is adorned with towering skyscrapers, while its streets are alive with local markets, street food stalls, and iconic landmarks. From the serene Marine Drive to the lively Colaba Causeway, Mumbai offers a kaleidoscope of experiences, making it a must-visit destination for travelers.

South & East Asia.name: Nagasaki
South & East Asia.overview: Nagasaki, located on the island of Kyushu, is a captivating blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Nestled among picturesque hills and overlooking a serene bay, the city offers stunning landscapes and vibrant urban life. Known as a gateway to Japan’s international interactions, Nagasaki features a unique fusion of Japanese and Western influences. The city boasts charming neighborhoods, bustling markets, and beautiful parks. With its warm hospitality and diverse attractions, Nagasaki provides a compelling experience for travelers seeking history, culture, and scenic tranquility.

South & East Asia.name: Osaka
South & East Asia.overview:
Osaka, Japan’s third-largest city, is a vibrant metropolis renowned for its modernity, culinary delights, and warm hospitality. Nestled on the island of Honshu, it serves as a major economic hub with a dynamic mix of urban innovation and cultural richness. Known as the "Nation's Kitchen," Osaka is a haven for food enthusiasts, offering everything from street food to gourmet dining. Its stunning skyline, iconic landmarks, and thriving entertainment districts make it a favorite destination for tourists seeking excitement and cultural immersion.

Japan’s second-largest city, Osaka, is the unofficial culinary capital of Japan. Its backstreets and laneways are full of restaurants and bars serving delicious local cuisine. Osaka is also home to some of the most famous castles, temples, and monuments from Japan’s past, just begging to be explored. The 16th-century Osaka Castle is a magnificent white and gold landmark not to be missed. First built in 728 the world heritage listed Todaiji Temple features the immense Great Buddha Statue and Sika deer that wander freely throughout the temple grounds.

South & East Asia.name: Phu My (Ho Chi Minh)
South & East Asia.overview:
Beautiful beaches. Lively cities. Fascinating culture. Food that sends your tastebuds to the stars and back. Prepare to be pleasantly surprised when you visit vibrant Vietnam. The port of Phu My (pronounced "Foo Me") is your gateway to Ho Chi Minh City and the seaside resort of Vung Tau.

A bustling city on the Mekong River, Ho Chi Minh City offers markets galore and, historical sites. The air is filled with the cries of street hawkers and honking horns. Bicycles, motorbikes and automobiles fly down the boulevards at dizzying speeds. And everywhere, friendly faces and warm greetings meet you.

South & East Asia.name: Port Klang (Kuala Lumpur)
South & East Asia.overview:
Port Kelang is your gateway to Malaysia’s modern and bustling Kuala Lumpur, a city fusing Malayan, Chinese and Indian cultures.  

With fascinating culture and history, plus shopping galore on offer, it might be hard to decide what to do with your time in Kuala Lumpur. Climbing the KL Tower offers stunning city views from the observation deck. The renowned Batu Caves are a complex of caves considered to be one of the most popular Hindu shrines outside of India. Here, visitors can marvel at the 141-foot tall gilded statue of the deity Lord Murugan guarding the 272 steps that lead to the majestic Cathedral Cave. Fans of retail therapy are also in luck, the shopping here is some of Asia's finest.

South & East Asia.name: Seogwipo (Jeju)
South & East Asia.overview: Seogwipo is your gateway to beautiful Jeju Island. As soon as you step foot on Jeju it will be easy to see why it’s often referred to as “The Hawaii of Korea”. Soaring Mount Hallasan, a dormant volcano covered in lush green vegetation dominates the stunning landscape which also features white sandy beaches, crystal-clear waters and winding coastal trails. Visitors can hike up the mountain for breathtaking views of the crater lake near its summit or visit Cheonjeyeon Waterfall - known as the Pond of God. You can admire the falls from the Seonimgyo arch bridge, which offers a spectacular view of the surrounding rock formations and verdant parkland. Jeju's Jungmung Saekdal Beach is also popular, nestled in a secluded spot surrounded by cliffs. Alternatively, visitors can explore Seogwipo Healing Forest offering a serene 11 km-long forest trail through cypress and cedar trees.

South & East Asia.name: Shanghai
South & East Asia.overview: Shanghai, China’s largest city, is a dynamic metropolis blending modern innovation with rich cultural heritage. Known for its futuristic skyline and vibrant lifestyle, it stands as a global financial hub and a symbol of China’s rapid urban development. Visitors are captivated by the bustling streets, diverse cuisine, and the seamless fusion of traditional charm and contemporary allure. From the historic Bund to the glittering skyscrapers of Lujiazui, Shanghai offers an unforgettable experience for travelers seeking adventure, culture, and world-class attractions in a city that never sleeps.

South & East Asia.name: Sihanoukville
South & East Asia.overview:
This bustling little seaside town is what tropical beach holiday dreams are made of. Along the beach (and by ‘along the beach’ we mean ‘right on the beach’) are numerous resorts, bars and restaurants serving up an incredibly relaxed atmosphere and cheap and delicious food and drink. 

The bay is specked with colourful local fishing boats, offshore islands offer excellent dive and snorkel sites, and fire twirlers, locals and tourists from all around the world come together to enjoy the stunning atmosphere, day and night.

South & East Asia.name: Singapore
South & East Asia.overview:
The Port of Singapore is a bustling hub of commerce and culture, located in the heart of Southeast Asia. As one of the world’s busiest ports, it seamlessly blends a vibrant maritime industry with a dynamic urban lifestyle. The port serves as a gateway to a multicultural city-state, offering visitors a chance to explore modern skyscrapers, lush green spaces, and rich culinary scenes. From its efficient infrastructure to its iconic skyline, the Port of Singapore provides a perfect starting point for tourists eager to discover the city’s endless attractions.

Embark on an unforgettable journey to Singapore with a Carnival Cruise. Each cruise getaway begins or ends with the Singapore Harbour, a destination that every cruise lover should experience. The cruise showcases the elegance of Singapore, with highlights including the world-renowned Orchard Road, the awe-inspiring Marina Bay, and the tranquil Botanical Gardens. This Southeast Asia cruise package promises to leave you spellbound. But with Carnival Cruise Line, the journey is about more than just the destination. It's about immersing yourself in exciting onboard activities, savouring a variety of dining options, and diving into the thrilling adventures you've always dreamed of on the best cruise ship.

South & East Asia.name: Tangalooma - Moreton Island
South & East Asia.overview: A Carnival Tangalooma, Moreton Island cruise from Sydney to Tangalooma Island Resort gives you access to one of Australia’s best-kept secrets. Experience snorkeling the famous shipwrecks or go whale watching for an experience you’ll never forget. A 4-day Tangalooma, Moreton Island Cruise from Sydney or Brisbane also offers the chance to take an exhilarating 4WD bus adventure around Tangalooma, Moreton Island, the 3rd largest sand island in the world! From sand tobogganing down the dunes, ATV quad bike tours and scenic helicopter flights to spotting turtles on a clear bottom kayak tour or even a spot of parasailing, this cruise has something for everyone. And enjoy more than 45 onboard activities on our Tangalooma, Moreton Island Cruise.

South & East Asia.name: Tanjung Priok, Jakarta, Indonesia
South & East Asia.overview: Indonesia is a sprawling nation made up of over 16,000 islands strewn across the Indian and Pacific oceans. But if you had to pick a big-deal attraction out of Indonesia’s lineup… definitely go with Jakarta, via the Tanjung Priok seaport. You might not guess that it’s one of the world’s most populated cities, and easily the biggest in Southeast Asia. But Jakarta wows with more than just its stats because its famously frenetic energy — fueled by cars, buses, motorcycle taxis, street food and, of course, its people — is just one side of the story. There’s also a side that owes its spirit to a history that goes way back to the days of the Dutch East Indies empire, and to the even-earlier Hindu kingdom. Don’t miss old-town Batavia, today called Kota Tua and home to preserved colonial buildings. Museum Wayang and Museum Nasional house important ancient artifacts, while Taman Mini Indonesian Indah is a different type of showcase, with this museum letting you get up-close to traditional houses from Indonesia’s various native peoples.

South & East Asia.name: Tokyo
South & East Asia.overview:
Tokyo, Japan's bustling capital, seamlessly blends ultramodern skyscrapers with historic temples, offering a dynamic urban landscape that captivates visitors. As a leading global metropolis, it serves as a hub for finance, culture, and technology, reflecting Japan's innovative spirit. The city's diverse districts, from the fashion-forward streets of Shibuya to the serene gardens of Chiyoda, provide a rich tapestry of experiences. Efficient public transportation, including the extensive subway system, ensures easy navigation for tourists. Tokyo's culinary scene is unparalleled, boasting numerous Michelin-starred restaurants alongside vibrant street food markets.

Like so many important port towns, Edo started off as a sleepy fishing village. This one, however, surprisingly grew up to become Japan’s capital, the biggest metro area on earth… and was later renamed “Tokyo.” For decades now it’s felt a bit like it’s living in the future… and you can probably say it is, with all the tech and neon and energy that flows throughout iconic neighborhoods like Akihabra and Shibuya. But with all the upgrades installed, Tokyo’s traditional culture hasn’t been replaced, best seen through the residential neighborhoods of narrow streets lined with traditional homes. And that’s not to mention the green spaces, like the city’s ample national parks, gardens, shrines and temples. Some can’t-miss spots are Imperial Palace Plaza in the city center, Tokyo Tower — which tops even the Eiffel Tower — plus the imposing Mount Fuji is always happy to join the city’s skyline for a photo.

South & East Asia.name: Victoria Harbour
South & East Asia.overview: Victoria Harbour, located between Hong Kong Island and the Kowloon Peninsula, is one of the world’s most iconic urban landscapes. Renowned for its breathtaking skyline and vibrant activity, the harbour is the heart of Hong Kong’s bustling trade and tourism industries. Visitors can enjoy stunning panoramic views, particularly during the Symphony of Lights show, where the skyscrapers are illuminated in a dazzling display. The harbour is also a hub for ferries and cruises, offering an unforgettable way to explore the city's dynamic waterfront.

South & East Asia.name: Xi'An
South & East Asia.overview: Xi’an, the capital of Shaanxi Province, is one of China’s most vibrant cities and a gateway to the country’s rich history. Located in central China, it serves as a hub connecting ancient traditions with modern progress. As the eastern terminus of the Silk Road, Xi’an is famed for its cultural significance, bustling markets, and exquisite culinary scene. Visitors are drawn to its blend of ancient relics and urban charm, from towering city walls to vibrant streets. Xi’an’s unique mix makes it an unforgettable destination.

South America.name: Brasília
South America.overview: Brasília, the capital of Brazil, is a city like no other, renowned for its modernist architecture and meticulously planned design. Situated in the heart of the country, Brasília was inaugurated in 1960 as a symbol of national progress and unity. Its unique layout, shaped like an airplane, features distinct zones for government, commerce, and living. The city is surrounded by lush savannas and boasts a wealth of green spaces, making it a haven for nature lovers and urban explorers alike.

South America.name: Buenos Aires
South America.overview:
Buenos Aires, the capital of Argentina, is a vibrant metropolis located on the western shore of the Río de la Plata. Known as the "Paris of South America," it boasts a rich cultural tapestry, blending European architectural styles with Latin American flair. 

 The city is divided into distinct neighborhoods, or "barrios," each offering unique experiences—from the historic charm of San Telmo to the upscale elegance of Recoleta. Buenos Aires is renowned for its tango music and dance, bustling café culture, and dynamic arts scene, making it a captivating destination for travelers.

If you mixed "old world charm" and "new world swagger," it would probably taste a little like dulce de leche... a beloved treat that Argentina is famous for inventing! Delicious as it is, food is just one of Argentina's claims to fame, and the local attractions and architecture keep up with the flavors just fine. Tango is another top export best enjoyed direct from the source, and the energetic Palermo Viejo nightlife is practically palpable during the day. This world city has been compared to Paris, and the history, old-fashioned confitería cafés and colonial architecture certainly all play their role, and despite all the modernity, plenty of green spaces remain.
